title: Architecture
text: verbs (tabular(), cols(), headers(), sort_rows(), subgroup(), paginate(), style(), preset()) accumulate immutable tabular_spec, S7 object. verb validates arguments, constructs one new property value, returns new spec property updated. Nothing rendered. specs immutable, base can branch many variants without interference, spec can inspected point pipe print().
code: sp <- tabular(saf_demo) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(align = "decimal")   )  is_tabular_spec(sp) #> [1] TRUE

title: Architecture
text: engine turns spec tabular_grid running six phases fixed order: sort — apply sort_rows() order (factor-aware, NA-last). headers — validate flatten header tree bands. style — resolve style() layers predicates per-cell nodes. format — apply per-column formatters, NA substitution, parse md() / html() inline markup. decimal — pad align = "decimal" columns using real font metrics. paginate — split rows (panels) pages per active preset’s geometry. result backend-agnostic: next phase differs per format. Inspect resolved grid without writing file using as_grid(): One Resolve, five Emits HTML preview RTF ship come resolved grid. numbers, structure, alignment, pagination decided Resolve; backend serialises . preview deliverable disagree.
code: g <- as_grid(sp) is_tabular_grid(g) #> [1] TRUE

title: Architecture
text: emit() runs Resolve, dispatches backend file’s extension (explicit format =):
code: emit(sp, "table.rtf")                 # by extension emit(sp, "table.out", format = "docx") # or force the format

title: Architecture
text: emit() can drop two -products alongside render, traceability: data_file — writes resolved wide data frame (post-engine) next render, QC can diff numbers actually displayed. Pass path function returns one. manifest = TRUE — writes CDISC ARS audit manifest describing display, using LinkML property names ARS logical data model. package boundary tabular owns display — resolve emit. never filters, aggregates, weights, inserts rows. boundary deliberate: keeps spec faithful description handed , exactly QC data_file ARS manifest document.
code: emit(   sp,   "t_14_1_1.rtf",   data_file = "t_14_1_1_qc.csv",   # resolved data for QC   manifest  = TRUE                  # t_14_1_1.audit.yml )

(N=%d)", saf["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total
(N=%d)",    saf["Total"]),    align = "decimal")   ) |>   headers("Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"))

title: Core concepts
text: tabular renderer, statistics engine. assumes numbers already computed laid way want display : one row input one row output, column value show. group_by, counting, percentages inside tabular. produce summary upstream — cards, gtsummary, dplyr, SAS — hand finished wide frame tabular(). common beginner mistake pass patient-level data (one row per subject), tabular faithfully render thousands rows. broken — exactly promises. Summarise first, render. Coming gtsummary rtables? packages compute summary render one step. tabular deliberately splits two: bring summary, get submission-grade output across five formats.

what: 2. The anatomy of a clinical table page
title: Core concepts
text: submission table just grid numbers. page four stacked sections, reviewers expect one place:  four-section page layout every tabular backend reproduces. Header section — protocol, optional status, page x y. Title lines — table number four centred title rows. Data section — optional subgroup banner, column-header band solid rules, data. Footnote lines — user footnotes, program path, name, timestamp. Every tabular verb maps onto piece picture: titles/ footnotes fill sections 2 4, headers() cols() build column band section 3, subgroup() adds banner, preset() controls page geometry frames four.

title: Core concepts
text: build table piping tabular_spec verbs. verb returns new spec one thing changed; previous spec never mutated. specs immutable, can branch common base several variants without surprises — safety table minimal-theme twin share base diverge last verb.
code: base <- tabular(saf_demo) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   )  is_tabular_spec(base) #> [1] TRUE

title: Core concepts
text: Nothing rendered pipe. work happens three phases:  Build spec, resolve grid, emit backend. Build — verbs (tabular(), cols(), headers(), …) accumulate immutable spec. Resolve — engine turns spec tabular_grid: sorts rows, flattens headers, applies styles, formats decimal-aligns cells, paginates. Emit — backend serialises grid RTF, PDF, HTML, LaTeX, DOCX. emit() runs Resolve Emit . inspect resolved grid without writing file, call as_grid(): One spec, five renderers Resolve backend-agnostic; Emit differs per format. HTML preview notebook RTF ship come resolved grid — numbers structure drift preview deliverable.
code: g <- as_grid(base) is_tabular_grid(g) #> [1] TRUE

title: Fonts & fidelity
text: backend resolves font fallback stack. check_fonts() reports fonts active stack actually installed machine returns per-backend stacks, can confirm submission font present batch run: missing font fatal — backend falls stack — submission want named font present rendered metrics match agency’s expectation.
code: sp <- tabular(saf_demo) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(align = "decimal")   )  stacks <- check_fonts(sp) #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = mono` #> backend: html #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> o monospace (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> x TeX Gyre Cursor (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Mono (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine) stacks$rtf #> [1] "Liberation Mono" "Courier New"     "Courier"

text: pivot_across() keeps division labour clean: cards (gtsummary, dplyr, SAS) computes summary; pivot_across() reshapes ; tabular() renders submission-grade output.

title: Rows, grouping & pagination
text: sort_rows() sets display order. Sort column — including hidden ones — engine applies Resolve.     Per-key direction hidden sort keys descending can vector matching , can sort one key another — sort_rows(= c("soc_n", "label"), descending = c(TRUE, FALSE)) gives canonical “system organ classes descending frequency, preferred terms alphabetically”. Sort keys frequently visible = FALSE columns exist drive order. Sorting factor-aware: factor column sorts level order, alphabetically, NAs sort last. CDISC best-overall- response column comes CR, PR, SD, PD order rather alphabetical.
code: # Display cells are formatted text ("217 (85.4)"), which would sort # lexically. Derive a hidden numeric key upstream and sort on that. ae <- saf_aeoverall ae$total_n <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total))  tabular(   ae,   titles = "Adverse-event categories, ordered by total frequency" ) |>   cols(     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Category"),     total_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = "total_n", descending = TRUE)

title: Rows, grouping & pagination
text: usage = "group" column turns bold section-header rows (group_display = "header_row"). row-grouping already saw Columns & headers: distinct group value introduces block rows.
code: tabular(saf_demo) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   )

title: Rows, grouping & pagination
text: group column stacks blocks within one table, subgroup() splits table separate, banner-labelled partitions — begins page print backends. Pass partitioning column(s) glue-style label template referencing columns:     sex × agegr combination becomes partition centred banner like F, <65. columns named label template hidden automatically. Group vs subgroup group (usage = "group") stacks blocks within one continuous table. subgroup (subgroup()) cuts table independent partitions start new page. Use group “Age, Sex, Race”; use subgroup “whole table, repeated per sex”.
code: tabular(   saf_subgroup,   titles = "Table 14.2.3  Vital Signs by Sex and Age Group" ) |>   cols(     sex        = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     sex_n      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param      = col_spec(usage = "group"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   subgroup(by = c("sex", "agegr"), label = "{sex}, {agegr}")

title: Rows, grouping & pagination
text: paginate() governs resolved table breaks across printed pages. set rows-per-page directly — engine computes row budget active preset (paper size, orientation, margins, font size) table’s chrome (titles, headers, footnotes). arguments : keep_together — names usage = "group" columns whose blocks must split across page break (keep system-organ class preferred terms). repeat_content — chrome repeats top/bottom every page; titles, headers, footnotes default. orphan_floor / widow_floor — minimum rows left bottom page / carried next, avoid stranded single rows. panels — split -wide table horizontal panels (groups columns) stacked page. continuation — optional “(continued)” marker carried-blocks. Pagination print concept HTML backend single continuous document, paginate() visible effect live previews — shapes RTF, PDF, DOCX output, pages real. Build preview HTML, emit paginated backend see splits.
code: tabular(saf_demo) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   paginate(     keep_together  = "variable",                          # never split a characteristic     repeat_content = c("titles", "headers", "footnotes"), # repeat chrome each page     orphan_floor   = 3,     widow_floor    = 2   )

title: Rows, grouping & pagination
text: Styling — target rows, groups, subgroup banners style(). Clinical cookbook — full AE--SOC/PT subgrouped vitals tables, sorted paginated end end. ```

title: Styling
text: .argument names surface. Everything else styling apply . Stack style() calls layer rules; later layers win per attribute.
code: base |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_group_headers())

title: Styling
code: base |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_group_headers()) |>   style(italic = TRUE, .at = cells_body(j = "Total")) |>   style(border_bottom = brdr("thick"), .at = cells_headers())

title: Styling
text: style() call accepts : Text — bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_family, font_size. Alignment — halign ("left"/"center"/"right"), valign. Borders — border_top, border_bottom, border_left, border_right, taking brdr(). Spacing — padding_top/_bottom/_left/_right, blank_above, blank_below. Surrounding text — pretext, posttext. brdr() describes one line. first argument width ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick", number points), line style, color:
code: base |>   style(border_top = brdr("thick"), border_bottom = brdr("thick"),         .at = cells_headers()) |>   style(border_bottom = brdr(width = "medium", style = "dashed"),         .at = cells_group_headers())

title: Styling
text: brdr() describes one line. first argument width ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick", number points), line style, color:
code: base |>   style(border_top = brdr("thick"), border_bottom = brdr("thick"),         .at = cells_headers()) |>   style(border_bottom = brdr(width = "medium", style = "dashed"),         .at = cells_group_headers())

title: Styling
text: cells_body(= ...) evaluates predicate data, can highlight cells content. Reference column — including hidden ones: Use j positional targeting instead predicate: cells_body(j = "Total") styles Total column, cells_body(= 1:3) first three rows.
code: base |>   style(background = "#eef6ff", .at = cells_body(where = stat_label == "n"))

what: Presets: cosmetic defaults for the whole table
title: Styling
text: style() targets specific cells, preset() carries table-wide geometry cosmetic defaults — paper size, orientation, font, five named-list “knobs” alignment, rules, fonts, colours, padding: c(...) knob shape cosmetic knob named list keyed surface, per-surface spec flat named vector — fonts = list(body = c(family = "Times New Roman", size = 9)), colors = list(header = c(text = "#1f2937", background = "#f3f4f6")), padding = list(body = c(top = 2, bottom = 2)). knobs strictly validated: unknown surfaces, unknown keys, nested lists rejected clear error. preset() applies one spec pipe. set_preset() sets session default every subsequent tabular() inherits — ggplot2::theme_set() analogue. get_preset() returns ; calling set_preset() arguments clears . preset_minimal() strips look single column-header rule normal-weight text throughout — spare house style groups prefer:
code: base |>   preset(     font_size   = 9,     orientation = "landscape",     rules  = list(midrule = brdr("thick")),     colors = list(header = c(text = "#1f2937"))   ) set_preset(font_size = 8)        # every later table starts at 8pt get_preset() #> <tabular::preset_spec> #>  @ font_size      : num 8 #>  @ font_family    : chr "mono" #>  @ orientation    : chr "landscape" #>  @ paper_size     : chr "letter" #>  @ margins        : num 1 #>  @ pagehead       : list() #>  @ pagefoot       : list() #>  @ indent_size    : int 2 #>  @ na_text        : chr "" #>  @ spacing        :List of 4 #>  .. $ title   : Named int [1:2] 1 1 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "above" "below" #>  .. $ body    : Named int [1:2] 0 0 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "above" "below" #>  .. $ subgroup: Named int [1:2] 0 0 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "above" "below" #>  .. $ footnote: Named int 0 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr "above" #>  @ stripe         : NULL #>  @ decimal_metrics: chr "chars" #>  @ decimal_markers: chr [1:5] "NR" "NE" "NC" "ND" "BLQ" #>  @ chrome_onscreen: chr "auto" #>  @ width_mode     : chr "content" #>  @ cell_padding   : num [1:2] 0 5.4 #>  @ style          : list() set_preset()                      # clear it again base |>   preset_minimal()

title: Styling
text: preset() applies one spec pipe. set_preset() sets session default every subsequent tabular() inherits — ggplot2::theme_set() analogue. get_preset() returns ; calling set_preset() arguments clears .
code: set_preset(font_size = 8)        # every later table starts at 8pt get_preset() #> <tabular::preset_spec> #>  @ font_size      : num 8 #>  @ font_family    : chr "mono" #>  @ orientation    : chr "landscape" #>  @ paper_size     : chr "letter" #>  @ margins        : num 1 #>  @ pagehead       : list() #>  @ pagefoot       : list() #>  @ indent_size    : int 2 #>  @ na_text        : chr "" #>  @ spacing        :List of 4 #>  .. $ title   : Named int [1:2] 1 1 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "above" "below" #>  .. $ body    : Named int [1:2] 0 0 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "above" "below" #>  .. $ subgroup: Named int [1:2] 0 0 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "above" "below" #>  .. $ footnote: Named int 0 #>  ..  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr "above" #>  @ stripe         : NULL #>  @ decimal_metrics: chr "chars" #>  @ decimal_markers: chr [1:5] "NR" "NE" "NC" "ND" "BLQ" #>  @ chrome_onscreen: chr "auto" #>  @ width_mode     : chr "content" #>  @ cell_padding   : num [1:2] 0 5.4 #>  @ style          : list() set_preset()                      # clear it again

title: Styling
text: preset_minimal() strips look single column-header rule normal-weight text throughout — spare house style groups prefer:
code: base |>   preset_minimal()

what: Packaging a house style with style_template()
title: Styling
text: submission renders dozens tables must share one identity. Build rules style_template() (chaining style() verb), attach preset every table inherits : Attach per session set_preset(.style = house) every subsequent table picks house style per-table style().
code: house <- style_template() |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_headers()) |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_group_headers()) |>   style(border_top = brdr("thick"), border_bottom = brdr("thick"),         .at = cells_headers())  is_style_template(house) #> [1] TRUE  base |>   preset(.style = house)

what: Inline markup with md() and html()
title: Styling
text: Wrap label, title, footnote, cell text md() Markdown (bold, italic, super/subscript) html() constrained HTML subset:     Body mass index kg/m2. cascade Styling resolves low--high priority: backend defaults → session preset (set_preset()) → spec preset (preset()) → per-spec style() layers. Later layers override earlier ones per attribute, NA fields leave prior value place. house style sets baseline per-table style() overrides just names.
code: tabular(   saf_demo,   titles = c(     md("**Table 14.1.1**"),     "Demographic Characteristics"   ),   footnotes = md("Body mass index in kg/m^2^.") ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   )

One short pipeline gives decimal alignment via real font metrics, multi-level column headers, predicate-targeted styling, group-aware pagination, built CDISC ADaM workflows FDA / EMA / PMDA submissions. R table package pairs live HTML preview paginated print deliverable: spec eyeball notebook one paginates RTF ship.

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: pipeline starts pre-summarised wide data frame (one row = one display row — tabular aggregation) chains one verb per concern. Every verb returns updated, immutable tabular_spec; engine resolves render time. tab renders markdown quick look (demographics table actual md backend output), self-contained HTML page, paginated PDF, tabularray LaTeX fragment, native OOXML .docx — one spec:
code: library(tabular)  # BigN denominators, keyed by arm n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short)  # columns render in data-frame order, so put them in dose order first demo <- saf_demo[c("variable", "stat_label", "placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")]  tab <- tabular(   demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"])   ),   footnotes = "Percentages are based on the number of subjects per treatment group." ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo (N=%d)",  n["placebo"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 (N=%d)",  n["drug_50"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 (N=%d)", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"),     Total      = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total (N=%d)",    n["Total"]),    align = "decimal")   )  # render to any backend by file extension (or format = "...") path <- emit(tab, tempfile(fileext = ".rtf"))   # submission deliverable

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: Five native backends, one spec. emit() dispatches file extension RTF 1.9.1, PDF (via tinytex), self-contained Bootstrap HTML, tabularray LaTeX, native OOXML DOCX. JVM, Office round-trip. Decimal alignment survives page. Numbers align decimal using backend’s real font metrics, guessed padding — columns stay aligned print, just screen. Submission chrome built . Multi-line titles, eleven footnote lines, page header/footer slots, four-section page layout regulatory reviewers expect. Group-aware pagination. Keep SOC preferred terms one page, repeat titles/headers/footnotes per page, control orphan/widow rows, split wide tables horizontal panels. Display-design. tabular styles renders; never filters, aggregates, weights. Pair cards / gtsummary / dplyr / SAS upstream feed tidy wide frame. QC trail. emit(data_file = ...) writes resolved wide data beside render, CDISC ARS audit manifest documents display.

what: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: Runs full engine pipeline spec returns resolved tabular_grid — intermediate object emit() hands backend. Pure function: files written, global state touched. Use development inspect emit() pass downstream, building custom backend, piping resolved grid non-file consumer (e.g. inline preview chunk Quarto notebook).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
code: as_grid(spec)

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: spec tabular_spec resolve. <tabular_spec>: required. Built verb chain (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset()).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: tabular_grid S7 object. Two slots: @pages — list one entry per display page. entry named list pagination fields (page_index, panel_index, is_continuation, continuation, show_titles, repeat_headers, show_footnotes_here), row + column slice indices (row_indices, col_indices, col_names), sliced cell text (cells_text — character matrix), sliced inline ASTs (cells_ast — list-matrix inline_ast), sliced style nodes (cells_style — list-matrix style_node), column-label ASTs visible columns (col_labels_ast). @metadata — per-table information backends consume per render: format (resolved backend tag, NA_character_ as_grid() calls), rows_per_page, total_pages, total_panels, nrow_data, ncol_data, col_names, cols (original col_spec() entries keyed column name), headers (flattened header band grid), titles, footnotes, titles_ast, footnotes_ast, col_labels_ast, pagehead_ast / pagefoot_ast (resolved page-band content — NULL active preset declares band, otherwise list(left, center, right) length-N lists inline_ast N = row count index 1 body-edge row).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: Engine pipeline order load-bearing. Phases run fixed order; order matters phase reads post- previous-phase state spec: engine_sort() — apply sort spec. engine_headers() — validate header tree flatten band grid. engine_style() — evaluate every style predicate post-sort data grid. predicate may reference column spec@data. engine_format() — apply per-column formats, substitute na_text, parse every cell / title / footnote / label parse_inline() inline_ast. engine_decimal() — column-wide decimal alignment column flagged col_spec(align = "decimal"). Operates formatted text; output character matrix NBSP padding inserted decimal marks line . engine_paginate() — split pages (vertical row chunks + horizontal panel chunks). plan drives per-page slicing cells / styles / ASTs . grid backend contract. Every backend (backend_md, future backend_html, etc.) consumes tabular_grid — never tabular_spec. New backends need walk grid@pages grid@metadata; engine pipeline fixed dependency never re-implement. /O. as_grid() writes nothing disk touches global state. safe call repeatedly interactive exploration; cost roughly one emit() without backend write step.

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
code:
# ---- Example 1: Demographics — inspect the resolved grid ---- # # Resolve the canonical safety-pop demographics pipeline into a # `tabular_grid` and inspect what `emit()` would hand a backend. # The first page's `cells_text` matrix is the decimal-aligned # output as the backend would render it; the metadata carries the # pagination plan + header / title / footnote ASTs. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short)  demo <- tabular(   saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"])   ),   footnotes = "Source: ADSL." ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo
N=%d",  n["placebo"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50
N=%d",  n["drug_50"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100
N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total
N=%d",    n["Total"]),    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label"))  demo_grid <- as_grid(demo) demo_grid@metadata$total_pages #> [1] 2 demo_grid@pages[[1]]$cells_text[1:3, c("stat_label", "placebo")] #>      stat_label    placebo         #> [1,] "Age (years)" ""              #> [2,] "  Mean (SD)" " 75.2 (8.59) " #> [3,] "  Median"    " 76.0        "  # ---- Example 2: AE-by-SOC/PT paginated grid — verify the split ---- # # Same shape as Example 1 plus pagination protecting the SOC # grouping. With a tight font size the grid carries multiple page # entries; concatenating each page's `row_indices` reconstructs # the full data, and every page carries the full header band grid # at `grid@metadata$headers` so backends can re-render the header # on every continuation page. ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total))  ae_spec <- tabular(   ae,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term",     sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"])   ),   footnotes = "Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(usage = "group", visible = FALSE,                         group_display = "column_repeat"),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo
N=%d",  n["placebo"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50
N=%d",  n["drug_50"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100
N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total
N=%d",    n["Total"]),    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) |>   paginate(keep_together = "soc")  ae_grid <- as_grid(ae_spec) length(ae_grid@pages) #> [1] 3  # ---- Example 3: Subgroup partition — one page set per group ---- # # When `subgroup()` is attached, `as_grid()` runs the resolve # pipeline once per group and concatenates the pages. `saf_subgroup` # carries `sex` as a natural partition axis; inspect # `@pages[[i]]$subgroup_index` and `@pages[[i]]$subgroup_line_ast` # to confirm each page knows its group identity and banner text. # `sex` auto-hides as the partition `by` column; no explicit # `col_spec(visible = FALSE)` needed. sg_spec <- tabular(saf_subgroup) |>   cols(     agegr      = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Age Group"),     sex_n      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param      = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic")   ) |>   subgroup("sex")  sg_grid <- as_grid(sg_spec) length(sg_grid@pages) #> [1] 2 vapply(sg_grid@pages, function(p) p$subgroup_index %||% NA_integer_, integer(1)) #> [1] 1 2  # ---- Example 4: Pre-flight inspection before emit() ---- # # Resolve a spec to its grid without writing anywhere. Useful in # tests, for snapshotting cell text under different presets, or # for spec-introspection inside higher-level wrappers that need # to know how many pages a render will produce. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) demog_spec <- tabular(   saf_demo,   titles = "Demographics" ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(       label = sprintf("Placebo
N=%d", n["placebo"]),       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50    = col_spec(       label = sprintf("Drug 50
N=%d", n["drug_50"]),       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100   = col_spec(       label = sprintf("Drug 100
N=%d", n["drug_100"]),       align = "decimal"     ),     Total      = col_spec(       label = sprintf("Total
N=%d", n["Total"]),       align = "decimal"     )   ) grid <- as_grid(demog_spec) length(grid@pages) #> [1] 2 dim(grid@pages[[1]]$cells_text) #> [1] 49  5

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: Build small immutable record describing one border line — width, style, colour. brdr() value stroke hand preset() rules knob (one entry per rule name, e.g. rules = list(midrule = brdr(width = 0.75))) style()'s border arguments (style(border_top = brdr(...), .= cells_table(side = "rows"))). Successive preset() calls layer cleanly, one-override composes onto house-style template without disturbing rules.

title: Border-line specification — brdr
code: brdr(width = "thin", style = "solid", color = "currentColor")  is_brdr(x)

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: width Stroke width. <numeric(1) | character(1)>: default "thin"*. Either numeric points (>= 0) one four named keywords ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick"`). style Line style. <character(1)>: default "solid"*. One "solid", "dashed", "dotted", "double", "dashdot", "none"`. color Stroke colour. <character(1)>: default "currentColor"*. Hex ("#RRGGBB"), CSS colour name, "currentColor"` inherit surrounding text colour. x R object — tested is_brdr() membership tabular_brdr S3 class.

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: tabular_brdr S3 object — length-3 named list suitable preset(rules = list(<rule> = .)) style(border_* = .).

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: Surface. single tabular_brdr value length-3 named list class "tabular_brdr": list(style, width, color). shape identical bare triple style()'s per-side scalars accept, resolver R/borders.R can ingest either form transparently. Construct brdr(); test is_brdr(). Width keywords. width accepts either numeric points (typical clinical values: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 1.5) one four named keywords: Keywords resolve numeric points immediately; constructed value carries numeric width. Numeric inputs pass unchanged non-negative check. Style enum. style one "solid" (default), "dashed", "dotted", "double", "dashdot", "none". "none" explicit clear--rule sentinel: setting rule brdr(style = "none") (bare string "none") preset()(rules = list(...)) suppresses baseline rule backend otherwise draw. Color. Hex ("#212529"), CSS colour name ("black", "slategray"), "currentColor" (default; resolves surrounding text colour per backend convention — w:color="auto" DOCX, document text colour RTF, CSS currentColor keyword HTML).

title: Border-line specification — brdr
code:
# ---- Example 1: A house-style rule set ---- # # The `rules` knob takes one brdr() value per rule name. Here a # thick column-label divider (midrule), a hairline dotted rule # between body rows (rowrule), and the muted spanner rule dropped. # Unlisted rules keep their booktabs defaults. demo_n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( saf_aeoverall, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Overall Summary of Adverse Events", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", demo_n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Subjects counted once per category." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Category"), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", demo_n["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", demo_n["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", demo_n["drug_100"])), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", demo_n["Total"])) ) |> preset( rules = list( midrule = brdr(width = "thick"), rowrule = brdr(width = "hairline", style = "dotted"), spanrule = "none" ) ) #> #>

# ---- Example 2: Wrap a custom style into a reusable function ---- # # The recommended way to share a rule style across many tables is to # wrap the `preset()` call in a small function. A later `preset()` / # `style()` call layers a one-off override cleanly on top. custom_style <- function(spec) { spec |> preset( rules = list( toprule = brdr(width = "thin", color = "#212529"), midrule = brdr(width = "thin", color = "#212529"), bottomrule = brdr(width = "thin", color = "#212529") ) ) } tabular(saf_n) |> custom_style() |> preset(rules = list(rowrule = brdr("hairline", "dashed"))) #> #>

# ---- Example 3: Width keyword vs numeric, every style enum value ---- # # Width accepts both the four named keywords and a bare numeric # in points; style accepts six enum values. Use `is_brdr()` to # confirm the constructor returned a valid `tabular_brdr` rather # than a fallback list. for (w in c("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick")) { cat(w, "=", brdr(width = w)$width, "pt ") } #> hairline = 0.25 pt #> thin = 0.5 pt #> medium = 1 pt #> thick = 1.5 pt is_brdr(brdr(width = 0.75)) #> [1] TRUE lapply( c("solid", "dashed", "dotted", "double", "dashdot", "none"), function(s) brdr(style = s) ) #> [[1]] #> 0.5pt solid currentColor #> #> [[2]] #> 0.5pt dashed currentColor #> #> [[3]] #> 0.5pt dotted currentColor #> #> [[4]] #> 0.5pt double currentColor #> #> [[5]] #> 0.5pt dashdot currentColor #> #> [[6]] #> 0.5pt none currentColor #> # ---- Example 4: A full grid via the body-edge style() path ---- # # The `rules` knob covers the named booktabs anatomy; for the body # outer frame and inter-column separators, hand brdr() to # `style(.at = cells_table(side = ...))`. Here a medium outer frame # plus hairline column separators on a demographics table. tabular(saf_demo, titles = "Demographics with a full grid") |> cols( variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"), stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"), placebo = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = "Drug 50", align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = "Total", align = "decimal") ) |> style(border = brdr(width = "medium"), .at = cells_table(side = "outer")) |> style(border_left = brdr("hairline"), .at = cells_table(side = "cols")) #> #>

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: Build tabular_location value naming one region rendered table; pass result style()'s argument. constructor targets one surface (body, headers, footnotes, ...); optional / j / / level / labels filters narrow target within surface.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
code: cells_body(i = NULL, j = NULL, where = NULL)  cells_headers(level = NULL, labels = NULL, j = NULL)  cells_group_headers(j = NULL, where = NULL)  cells_title()  cells_subgroup_labels()  cells_footnotes()  cells_pagehead(slot = NULL)  cells_pagefoot(slot = NULL)  cells_table(side = NULL, i = NULL, j = NULL)  is_tabular_location(x)

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: Row index filter. <integer | logical | character | NULL>. Integer = 1-based row numbers; logical = length-nrow mask (broadcasts scalar TRUE/FALSE); character = matches visible row labels. NULL (default) = filter (every row). j Column index filter. <integer | character | NULL>. Integer = 1-based column positions; character = matches column names spec@data. NULL (default) = every column. Predicate. unquoted expression evaluating length-nrow logical vector run data grid. Captured rlang quosure (pvalue < 0.05 works without needing wrap vars() similar). Mutually exclusive . level Header-band depth (cells_headers). <integer(1) | NULL>. 1 = topmost spanner band; increasing integers walk toward leaves. -1 = leaf band (per-column labels built col_spec@label). NULL (default) = every band every depth. labels Header-band labels (cells_headers). <character | NULL>. Targets header_node(s) whose @label matches, depth. Mutually exclusive level. slot Band slot (cells_pagehead / cells_pagefoot). <character(1) | NULL>. One "left", "center", "right", NULL every slot. side Table edge / separator (cells_table). <character(1) | NULL>. One "outer" (four outer edges), "outer_top", "outer_bottom", "outer_left", "outer_right", "rows" (horizontal separator body rows), "cols" (vertical separator body columns), NULL whole-body (cells_body()). x R object — tested is_tabular_location() membership tabular_location S3 class.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: tabular_location S3 list slots surface, , j, , labels, level, slot, side (unused slots NULL). Pass style()'s argument.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
text: One surface per location. tabular_location always names exactly one : body, headers, group_headers, title, subgroup_labels, footnotes, pagehead, pagefoot, table. Cross-surface styling layers via multiple chained style() calls (one per location). Index vocabulary. supported, (rows) j (columns) arguments accept integer, logical, character vectors — matching convention established flextable (bold(ft, , j)) tinytable (style_tt(, j)). Character vectors match data frame's column names (j) row labels (); integers 1-based positions; logicals broadcast nrow / ncol. Predicate vocabulary. cells_body(= pvalue < 0.05) canonical data-driven filter — captured rlang quosure evaluated engine time post-sort grid. Mutually exclusive (target either index predicate, ). cells_headers cells_column_spanners. verb builds multi-level header tree named headers(). location follows vocabulary: one word ("headers") covers entire column-header section — inner spanner bands leaf band per-column labels. Pass level labels narrow.

title: Cell-location constructors for style() — cells
code: # Whole body cells (the default for style()) cells_body() #> <tabular_location: body()>  # Row index 1:3, column "Total" cells_body(i = 1:3, j = "Total") #> <tabular_location: body(i=1,2,3, j=Total)>  # Data-driven subset cells_body(where = stat_label == "Mean (SD)") #> <tabular_location: body(where=stat_label == "Mean (SD)")>  # Topmost spanner band only cells_headers(level = 1) #> <tabular_location: headers(level=1)>  # Leaf band (per-column labels) cells_headers(level = -1) #> <tabular_location: headers(level=-1)>  # A specific spanner by label cells_headers(labels = "Treatment Group") #> <tabular_location: headers(labels=c('Treatment Group'))>  # Section-header rows for col_spec(group_display = "header_row") cells_group_headers() #> <tabular_location: group_headers()>  # Title / footnotes blocks cells_title() #> <tabular_location: title()> cells_footnotes() #> <tabular_location: footnotes()>  # Page-header / page-footer slots cells_pagehead(slot = "left") #> <tabular_location: pagehead(slot='left')> cells_pagefoot(slot = "right") #> <tabular_location: pagefoot(slot='right')>  # Outer table frame cells_table(side = "outer") #> <tabular_location: table(side='outer')>  # Horizontal rules between body rows cells_table(side = "rows") #> <tabular_location: table(side='rows')>

what: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: Walks resolved font fallback chain backend reports entries local machine can find. Useful answering "preview seeing fonts downstream reviewer see?".

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
code: check_fonts(spec)

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: spec tabular_spec preset_spec. <tabular_spec | preset_spec>: required. spec whose effective preset determines font chain walk.

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: Invisibly returns resolved per-backend chains named list character vectors. Side effect: prints cli tree showing availability marker every entry.

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
text: diagnostic change emit() writes file. Tabular's backends emit font names (CSS strings, LaTeX \setmainfont commands, RTF font-table entries); consuming application (browser, LaTeX engine, Word, Adobe Reader) opening machine resolves names installed fonts. check_fonts() purely informational — tells entries cross-platform fallback chain can see machine, can predict drift. Status markers: v — font installed machine (via systemfonts). o — font CSS / LaTeX generic; always resolvable consuming application. x — font installed machine; consuming app different machine may may . Requires systemfonts package (Suggests); call install.packages("systemfonts") first installed.

title: Check font availability across backends — check_fonts
code: # ---- Example 1: Inspect default font resolution ---- # # Build a spec with the default font_family ("mono") and ask # which entries in the cross-platform chain are findable # locally. Useful before sharing a render with downstream # reviewers who may be on a different OS. spec <- tabular(   saf_demo,   titles = "Demographics" ) if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   check_fonts(spec) } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = mono`  #> backend: html #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> o monospace (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> x TeX Gyre Cursor (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Mono (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine)  # ---- Example 2: Diagnose a Courier New request ---- # # A request for "Courier New" (a specific named font) renders # on macOS / Windows but may fall back to a serif on Linux. # `check_fonts()` flags this so the user knows to switch to # the "mono" generic for portable output. spec_mono <- tabular(   saf_demo,   titles = "Mono request" ) |>   preset(font_family = "Courier New") if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   check_fonts(spec_mono) } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = Courier New`  #> backend: html #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> o monospace (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine) #> x TeX Gyre Cursor (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Mono (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Liberation Mono (not on this machine) #> v Courier New #> x Courier (not on this machine)  # ---- Example 3: Explicit cross-platform stack ---- # # A length>1 input is treated as an explicit fallback chain and # emitted verbatim — no alias lookup, no fabrication. Use this # when the first choice is a sponsor / brand face that needs an # honest fallback for reviewers who don't have it installed. spec_brand <- tabular(saf_demo) |>   preset(font_family = c("Inter", "Liberation Sans", "Arial", "sans")) if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   check_fonts(spec_brand) } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = Inter          , Liberation Sans, Arial          , and sans           `  #> backend: html #> x Inter (not on this machine) #> x Liberation Sans (not on this machine) #> v Arial #> o sans (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Inter (not on this machine) #> x Liberation Sans (not on this machine) #> v Arial #> o sans (generic, always available) #> backend: rtf #> x Inter (not on this machine) #> x Liberation Sans (not on this machine) #> v Arial #> o sans (generic, always available)  # ---- Example 4: Compare serif vs sans fallback chains ---- # # Side-by-side check of the two generic families. Useful when # deciding the house-style default: the serif chain leads with # Liberation Serif (Linux-server-first); the sans chain leads # with Liberation Sans. Both close with the backend's native # fallback layer (CSS generic on HTML, Latin Modern on LaTeX). if (requireNamespace("systemfonts", quietly = TRUE)) {   tabular(saf_demo) |>     preset(font_family = "serif") |>     check_fonts()    tabular(saf_demo) |>     preset(font_family = "sans") |>     check_fonts() } #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = serif`  #> backend: html #> x Liberation Serif (not on this machine) #> v Times New Roman #> x Times (not on this machine) #> o serif (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Liberation Serif (not on this machine) #> v Times New Roman #> x Times (not on this machine) #> x TeX Gyre Termes (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Roman (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Liberation Serif (not on this machine) #> v Times New Roman #> x Times (not on this machine) #>  #> ── Font resolution for `font_family = sans`  #> backend: html #> x Liberation Sans (not on this machine) #> v Arial #> v Helvetica #> o sans-serif (generic, always available) #> backend: latex #> x Liberation Sans (not on this machine) #> v Arial #> v Helvetica #> x TeX Gyre Heros (not on this machine) #> x Latin Modern Sans (not on this machine) #> backend: rtf #> x Liberation Sans (not on this machine) #> v Arial #> v Helvetica

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: Build single column's display attributes — usage, label, format, visibility, width, alignment, NA text. result feeds cols(), stamps input column name onto spec named- argument position attaches parent tabular_spec.

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
code: col_spec(   usage = NULL,   label = NA_character_,   format = NULL,   visible = TRUE,   width = "auto",   group_display = "header_row",   group_skip = NA,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL,   na_text = "",   indent_by = NA_character_ )

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: usage Engine role. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. One : "display" (default cols()) — pass-. "group" — row-label repeat-suppression continuation-page repeat keys. Use variable, soc, stat_label. "indent" — prefix every body cell column one indent level (preset@indent_size space-widths). Composes additively indent_by (column gets depth_by + 1 indent levels per row). Backends native padding-left semantics (HTML / LaTeX / RTF / DOCX / PDF) emit cell padding wrapped continuation lines align indented baseline; Markdown carries literal space-prefix. Synthesised group-header rows (group_display = "header_row") NEVER indented — parent depth 0. "id" — row-identifier column. Renders like "display" (one value per row, never collapses) joins stub: repeats every horizontal panel (paginate(panels = N)) shows left continuous backend (HTML / Markdown) collapses panels one table. PROC REPORT ID role, orthogonal grouping. Use per-row statistic label ("n", "Mean", "SD") must stay legible every panel wide demographics efficacy table. NULL — inferred "display" cols().       label Display label column header. <character(1)>: default NA_character_. Embed \n multi-line headers (arm name row 1, BigN denominator row 2 clinical convention). NA_character_ means use input column name verbatim. Restriction: Empty string whitespace-labels accepted , unlike headers() band labels strict.   format Post-cell formatter. <character(1) | function | NULL>: default NULL. sprintf template applied per cell, unary function(x) -> character length, NULL backend default. Restriction: Character templates probed sprintf(format, 0) construction; malformed templates fail fast. Tip: Use function non-sprintf formatting (locale- aware numbers, thousand separators, conditional symbols).   visible Whether column renders. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. FALSE hides column output keeps spec@data sort_rows() style() predicates can still reference . Interaction: Hidden columns standard pattern sort-key helpers (row_type, n_total) numeric counts behind formatted-text percentage cells. Auto-hide. depth column named indent_by every column named subgroup(= ...) referenced via {col} placeholder subgroup banner template flipped visible = FALSE automatically engine time — restating redundant. Break-group column. hidden usage = "group" column emits header rows -column text; contributes group_skip transitions, group_display ignored hidden. canonical "spacer" drops blank line wherever marker value changes (e.g. continuous stats vs. categorical groups inside one characteristic): col_spec(usage = "group", group_skip = TRUE, visible = FALSE). width Column width — auto-sized, pinned, proportional. <character(1) | numeric(1)>: default "auto". "auto" (default) — engine measures widest cell (header + body) using bundled Adobe AFM Core 13 glyph metrics distributes available content width. header sized widest word, multi-word header (e.g. "n, median") wraps spaces; non-breaking space ( ) keeps run whole. body sized widest line never wraps, numeric values stay intact. Pin numeric width wrap body . <number> — pinned inches. Backends wrap content inside pinned width (tabularray Q[wd=...], HTML style="width:...", RTF / DOCX twips conversion). "2.5in" / "60mm" / "4cm" / "30pt" / "5pc" — pinned dimension explicit TeX unit. behaviour bare numeric. "30%" — proportional width, percent available content width. Resolved engine time printable area. Tip: Mix freely. Pinned percent widths take priority; "auto" columns distribute whatever space remains. pinned widths together exceed available content width, engine warns leaves "auto" columns natural fit (layout may overflow). Restriction: Must positive. Percent values must fall [0, 100]. Font-relative units (em, ex, rem) rejected (font-size context parse time). Cross-format semantics (gt convention). width value user's source--truth. HTML emits verbatim <col style="width:..."> (CSS accepts every unit: %, , px, pt, cm, mm). Paper backends (LaTeX / RTF / PDF / DOCX) convert native unit via AFM / distribute-widths pipeline. HTML unconditionally responsive: width = "auto" (default), browser auto-sizes column cells wrap viewport narrows. Note: NA NULL rejected. pre-v0.1.0 tabular NA deferred backend auto-fit; path inconsistent across backends replaced "auto" default, produces identical widths across RTF / LaTeX / HTML. group_display usage = "group" values render body. <character(1)>: default "header_row". Active usage = "group"; ignored otherwise. "header_row" (default) — unique value emits section header row block data rows. source column hidden visible body. Matches canonical submission Appendix shape used clinical TFL house templates (Disposition, Demographics, Statistical Report sections). "column" — column stays visible; repeated values suppressed (first row value shows label). PROC REPORT's default grouping variables. "column_repeat" — column stays visible; every row repeats value (suppression). shape R's print.data.frame produces. Composition multiple group columns. one usage = "group" column declared, FIRST one encountered cols() order outer group; subsequent group columns nest inside . column's group_display choice independent — common clinical pattern outer variable "header_row" plus inner stat_label "column" (visible row labels section header).   group_skip Insert blank row consecutive groups. <logical(1)>: default NA. Active usage = "group"; ignored otherwise. Three values: TRUE — engine injects one blank row immediately value transition column (PROC REPORT's BREAK var / SKIP semantics, lifted per-column control). Never trails final group. FALSE — never insert blank row column. NA (default) — follow group_display: TRUE group_display = "header_row", FALSE "column" "column_repeat". Picks canonical Appendix-shape without extra knob set. Interaction: two columns effective group_skip = TRUE value transitions coincide row, engine emits ONE blank row boundary, one per column. Transition row indices unioned across contributing group columns.   align Horizontal alignment within column. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. One : "left" — character columns; row labels. "center" — column-header band; rarely data cells. "right" — numeric content without decimals. "decimal" — numeric mixed-format cells aligned decimal mark. Use "5 (3.2%)" next "54 (32.1%)". NULL (default) — falls preset(alignment = list(body_halign = ...)) baked default "left". Tip: "decimal" pads numerics non-breaking spaces decimal mark falls single column-wide anchor. active preset's decimal_metrics knob reserved future em-aware padding refinement (see preset()); current engine pads character count. Default behaviour. align unset (NULL / NA), every column emits body left-aligned header centred, regardless column's R data type. tabular's canonical input pre-summarised wide data frames numeric content already formatted character strings (e.g. "52 (60.5)"), .numeric()-based auto-detection mis-classify columns text align left — opposite intent. Use explicit align = "decimal" NBSP-padded numeric columns (centred header padded centroid) align = "right" plain right-aligned numeric columns. default cascade body → preset(alignment = list( body_halign = ...)) → CSS text-align: left; header → preset(alignment = list(header_halign = ...)) → CSS text-align: center. valign Vertical alignment within cell. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. One "top", "middle", "bottom". NULL falls preset(alignment = list(body_valign = ...)) (baked default "top"). Per-cell overrides via style(valign = ...) still win column setting. Tip: Set "middle" row-label column banded- row table label stays centred multi-line stat-block adjacent cell. na_text Text substituted NA cells. <character(1)>: default "". Substituted format step, format need anticipate NA. Tip: Use sentinel ("-", "NR", ".") blank cells ambiguous, e.g. "applicable" "reported" render blank. indent_by Name column spec@data whose per-row integer / logical values drive indent depth column. <character(1)>: default NA_character_. set, engine reads spec@data[[indent_by]] prefixes column's text AST row strrep(" ", preset@indent_size * depth). referenced depth column auto-hidden — need set visible = FALSE . Typical SOC / PT pattern (bundled saf_aesocpt ships canonical depth column already attached, upstream construction needed):   Multi-depth nesting works way — values c(0L, 1L, 2L, …) produce 0, 1, 2, … indent levels preset@indent_size space-widths . Negative values clamp 0 (warn); fractional numerics floor (warn); NA → 0 (silent). Composes orthogonally group_display = "header_row": synthetic group headers (depth 0) stay flush parents; data rows carry column's declared depth. Works flat listings — indent_by require usage = "group" columns.
code: # Two row-label columns and four arm columns. cols(   variable   = col_spec(usage = "group"),   stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group"),   placebo    = col_spec(),   drug_50    = col_spec() ) # Section-band table: the `group_label` column drives section # headers; `stat_label` body rows auto-indent under each header # without an explicit depth column. cols(   group_label = col_spec(usage = "group",  group_display = "header_row"),   stat_label  = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "Response"),   placebo     = col_spec(align = "decimal") ) # End-to-end ARD → wide → tabular pipeline. The cards ARD # `saf_demo_card` is the long upstream input; `pivot_across()` # widens to one column per arm and stamps an internal marker # so [`sort_rows()`] can reject sort keys on those arm columns. # `cols()` then attaches per-column display rules. wide <- pivot_across(   saf_demo_card,   statistic = list(     continuous  = c(N = "{N}", "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})"),     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ) ) tabular(wide, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable                 = col_spec(       usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"     ),     stat_label               = col_spec(       usage = "group", label = "Statistic"     ),     Placebo                  = col_spec(align = "decimal"),     `Xanomeline High Dose`   = col_spec(       label = "High Dose", align = "decimal"     ),     `Xanomeline Low Dose`    = col_spec(       label = "Low Dose", align = "decimal"     ),     Total                    = col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) # Two-line header with arm name and BigN from saf_n. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) col_spec(   label = sprintf("Placebo\nN=%d", n["placebo"]),   align = "decimal" ) # sprintf template vs. function form. col_spec(format = "%.1f") col_spec(format = function(x) formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 1, big.mark = ",")) # Demographics layout: variable as section header, stat_label # as visible suppressed column. cols(   variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row"),   stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "column"),   placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal") ) # Default: header_row mode auto-injects blanks between sections. col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row")  # Override: keep the column visible (suppressed-value mode) but # still insert blank-row separators between value changes. col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "column", group_skip = TRUE)  # Override: section headers without the blank-row separator # (denser layout, used when vertical space is tight). col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row", group_skip = FALSE)  # Break-only "spacer": pairs with visible = FALSE to drop a blank # line wherever a hidden marker changes, without rendering the # column or any header row. group_display is ignored when hidden. col_spec(usage = "group", group_skip = TRUE, visible = FALSE) cols(   label    = col_spec(label = "Category", indent_by = "indent_level"),   soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),   row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE) )

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: col_spec S7 object. Pass cols() keyed input column name; constructor stamp name.

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
text: Constructor-. col_spec() know input column belongs cols() stamps name. Build reusable specs ordinary R objects (e.g. arm_col <- col_spec(align = "decimal")) apply multiple inputs without restating name. Merge semantics across repeated cols() calls. cols() called twice column, engine merges field--field: non-default value new spec overrides; default-valued field (NA / NULL / "" / TRUE) leaves existing field intact. Build column's spec stages without re-stating earlier attributes. Validation timing. Argument shapes validated eagerly — malformed sprintf template probed construction (sprintf(format, 0)) fails fast write time, render time.

title: Per-column display specification — col_spec
code:
# ---- Example 1: Demographics with every col_spec field exercised ---- # # Demographics table where every `col_spec` field is in play: # the row-label columns are pinned to a fixed width and aligned # left, the four arm columns embed BigN inline in the header, # decimal-align numeric content, and render `NA` cells as "-". n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( saf_demo, titles = c( "Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Percentages based on N per treatment group." ) |> cols( variable = col_spec( usage = "group", label = "Parameter", width = 2.0, align = "left" ), stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic", align = "left"), placebo = col_spec( label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal", na_text = "-" ), drug_50 = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal", na_text = "-" ), drug_100 = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal", na_text = "-" ), Total = col_spec( label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"]), align = "decimal", na_text = "-" ) ) |> sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label")) #> #>

#>
# ---- Example 2: AE table with indented label + hidden helpers ---- # # AE-by-SOC/PT table where `label` carries SOC and PT text under # one column, indented by `indent_level`. Hidden helpers # (`row_type`, `n_total`) drive the sort while staying off the # rendered page. Demonstrates `indent_by` plus `visible = FALSE` # for sort-only columns, fixed width on the wide label column, and # decimal alignment on all four arm columns. ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) tabular( ae, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ) ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / Preferred Term", indent_by = "indent_level", width = 2.5), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"]), align = "decimal") ) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) #> #>

# ---- Example 3: Format string + na_text for clean numeric display ---- # # `eff_estimates` ships four competing efficacy models with # pre-computed numeric estimates, 95% CI bounds (NA on the MMRM # row), and a nominal p-value. `format =` pins the printed # precision; `na_text` renders the missing CI bounds as a dash # rather than a literal "NA". `valign = "top"` keeps the multi- # line cell text aligned to the top. tabular(eff_estimates, titles = "Treatment-effect estimates by model") |> cols( model = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Model", valign = "top"), estimate = col_spec(label = "Estimate", align = "decimal", format = "%.2f"), lower_ci = col_spec( label = "Lower 95% CI", align = "decimal", format = "%.2f", na_text = "--" ), upper_ci = col_spec( label = "Upper 95% CI", align = "decimal", format = "%.2f", na_text = "--" ), p_value = col_spec( label = "p-value", align = "decimal", format = "%.4f" ) ) #> #>

# ---- Example 5: Non-collapsing `id` stub for a panelled table ---- # # `usage = "id"` marks `stat_label` ("n", "Mean", "SD", ...) as a # row identifier: like `display` it shows on every row, but it also # joins the stub, so it repeats on each horizontal panel created by # `paginate(panels = 2)`. On HTML / Markdown (no page width) the # panels collapse into one scrollable table with a "Panel 1 / Panel # 2" header note; on RTF / Word each panel is its own page with the # `variable` + `stat_label` stub repeated. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( saf_demo, titles = c("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics", "Safety Population") ) |> cols( variable = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "column", label = "Parameter"), stat_label = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "Statistic"), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"]), align = "decimal") ) |> paginate(panels = 2) #> #>

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: Add col_spec() entries tabular_spec. named argument one column: name input column .spec@data value col_spec carrying column's display attributes (usage, label, format, alignment, width, visibility, NA text). Columns mentioned get default col_spec() (usage = display) engine-validate time.

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
code: cols(.spec, ...)

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: .spec tabular_spec extend. <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind short user-supplied name (e.g. s, sp) ... spec slot. Pipe input (tabular(...) |> cols(...)) works normal way — spec supplied positionally. ... Named col_spec objects, one per column. name input column name .spec@data. Names must match existing column — pre-compute derived columns upstream dplyr::mutate() (equivalent) tabular(). Restriction: Names must unique within single cols() call (duplicates warn; "last value wins"). Tip: override attribute already declared, use second cols() call downstream let merge rule apply.

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining headers(), sort_rows(), style().

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: Sparse declaration. Declare columns whose attributes differ default — typical pipeline uses one cols() call one entry per non-default column. Within-call duplicates warn. duplicate name inside one cols() call warns "last value wins". intentionally override attribute, use second cols() call downstream let merge rule apply.

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
text: cols() called column, engine merges new col_spec existing one field-- field. non-default value new spec overrides; default- valued field leaves existing field intact. lets build column's spec stages — declare label--alignment block front, add width know fits, attach sort key, without re-stating earlier attributes. Essential generating specs programmatically (looping arms, layering house-style helper). Default values override existing field:
code: # Three-stage build: label/usage first, alignment second, width # third. Each stage leaves earlier fields intact. tabular(saf_demo) |>   cols(variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter")) |>   cols(variable = col_spec(align = "left")) |>   cols(variable = col_spec(width = 2.0)) # Result: variable has usage="group", label="Parameter", #         align="left", width=2.0 — all four fields set.

title: Attach per-column specifications — cols
code:
# ---- Example 1: Demographics with arm BigN inline in headers ---- # # Demographics table where the row-label columns sit on the left # and the four treatment-arm columns embed BigN in the header # label (drawn inline from the bundled `saf_n` data frame). Every # arm column is decimal-aligned so mixed-format cells like # "5 (3.2%)" line up on the decimal mark. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( saf_demo, titles = c( "Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Percentages based on N per treatment group." ) |> cols( variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"), stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"]), align = "decimal") ) |> sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label")) #> #>
#>

# ---- Example 2: BOR table with CDISC factor ordering and hidden helper ---- # # Best Overall Response table where `stat_label` carries the # canonical CDISC factor levels (driving the sort) and `row_type` # is hidden — present in the data for the sort, absent from the # rendered output via `col_spec(visible = FALSE)`. bor_levels <- c( "CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "Objective Response Rate (CR + PR)", "Disease Control Rate (CR + PR + SD)" ) eff <- eff_resp eff$stat_label <- factor(eff$stat_label, levels = bor_levels) ne <- stats::setNames(eff_n$n, eff_n$arm_short) tabular( eff, titles = c( "Table 14.2.1", "Best Overall Response and Response Rates", sprintf("Efficacy Evaluable Population (N=%d)", ne["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Response"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", ne["placebo"]), align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", ne["drug_50"]), align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", ne["drug_100"]), align = "decimal") ) |> sort_rows(by = "stat_label") #> #>

#>
# ---- Example 3: AE-by-SOC/PT with indented label + repeat-call merge ---- # # `label` carries SOC text on SOC rows and PT text on PT rows; # `indent_by = "indent_level"` indents the PT rows one level under # their SOC. `soc`, `row_type`, and `n_total` ride along as hidden # sort keys. A second `cols()` call later in the chain adds widths # once the user knows the page geometry; the repeat-call merge # preserves prior attributes (label, indent_by, align, visible) # without restating them. ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) tabular( ae, titles = c("Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term") ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = "Drug 50", align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = "Total", align = "decimal") ) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) |> # Second `cols()` call: add widths after the rest of the spec # is built. Repeat-call merge preserves prior attributes. cols( label = col_spec(width = "2.5in"), placebo = col_spec(width = "0.9in"), drug_50 = col_spec(width = "0.9in"), drug_100 = col_spec(width = "0.9in"), Total = col_spec(width = "0.9in") ) #> #>

# ---- Example 4: Compact AE-overall with pre-derived Active column ---- # # Drop the per-arm columns and surface only the Total. Pre-compute # the pooled "Active" column upstream (here `paste0(drug_50, " / ", # drug_100)`) before piping into `tabular()`; `cols()` then just # declares each column's display role. The same pattern handles # any post-pivot derivation (`pivot_across() |> mutate(...) |> # tabular()`). ae <- saf_aeoverall ae$active <- paste0(ae$drug_50, " / ", ae$drug_100) tabular( ae, titles = c("Table 14.3.0", "Adverse Event Overview"), footnotes = "Active = pooled Drug 50 + Drug 100 columns." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = ""), placebo = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal"), active = col_spec(label = "Active arms"), drug_50 = col_spec(visible = FALSE), drug_100 = col_spec(visible = FALSE), Total = col_spec(label = "Total", align = "decimal") ) #> #>

title: Treatment-effect estimates by model — eff_estimates
text: Four competing efficacy models treatment-effect point estimate, 95% confidence-interval bounds, nominal p-value. Shaped numeric-cell table (one row per model) rather usual pre-formatted character cells, exercises col_spec(format = ...) + col_spec(na_text = ...) cascade. One row (MMRM) carries NA CI bounds demonstrate na_text.

what: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — eff_resp
title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — eff_resp
text: Pre-summarised efficacy table. Per-arm counts best overall response (BOR) per CDISC category, plus derived ORR, CBR, DCR rate rows followed exact (Clopper-Pearson) 95% CI row. Four sections (Best Overall Response, Objective Response Rate, Clinical Benefit Rate, Disease Control Rate) encoded via groupid + group_label pair single usage = "group" / group_display = "header_row" group_label synthesises one bold section band per groupid block; body rows render band via usage = "indent" stat_label.

title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — eff_resp
code: eff_resp

title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — eff_resp
text: data frame 13 rows 7 columns: stat_label Row label ("CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "ORR (CR + PR)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)", "CBR (CR + PR + SD)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)", "DCR (CR + PR + SD + NON-CR/NON-PD)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)"). row_type "category" BOR categorical rows, "derived" ORR / CBR / DCR rate rows, "ci" paired confidence-interval rows. Hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE). placebo, drug_50, drug_100 Per-arm cell text ("n (pct)" rate rows, "(lower, upper)" CI rows). groupid Integer section id (1 = Best Overall Response, 2 = Objective Response Rate, 3 = Clinical Benefit Rate, 4 = Disease Control Rate). Hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE); used section sort / partition key. group_label Character section label, repeating across every row groupid block ("Best Overall Response" x7, "Objective Response Rate" x2, ...). Drives engine's usage = "group" header_row synthesis paired group_display = "header_row".

title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — eff_resp
text: Derived data-raw/bundle-demo.R pharmaverseadam::adrs_onco filtered PARAMCD == "BOR".

# 95% efficacy pattern: four bold section bands (Best Overall # Response / Objective Response Rate / Clinical Benefit Rate / # Disease Control Rate), each followed by indented stat rows. The # source already ships in the right display order, so no sort step # is needed; `group_label` repeats across every row of its section # so the engine's `header_row` mode emits exactly one band per # section. ne <- stats::setNames(eff_n$n, eff_n$arm_short) tabular( eff_resp, titles = c( "Table 14.2.1", "Best Overall Response and Response Rates", sprintf("Efficacy Evaluable Population (N=%d)", ne["Total"]) ) ) |> cols( group_label = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row"), stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "Response"), groupid = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec( label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", ne["placebo"]), align = "decimal" ), drug_50 = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", ne["drug_50"]), align = "decimal" ), drug_100 = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", ne["drug_100"]), align = "decimal" ) ) #> #>
