what: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: Resolve spec engine pipeline, dispatch backend registered chosen format, (optionally) write QC data file CDISC ARS audit manifest alongside rendered artefact. emit() package's terminal verb — returns file invisibly call can sit bottom pipe without losing path.

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
code: emit(spec, file, format = NULL, data_file = NULL, manifest = FALSE)

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: spec tabular_spec render. <tabular_spec>: required. full verb chain (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset()) feeds emit()'s first argument pipe. file Destination path rendered artefact. <character(1)>: required. Extension drives backend (see dispatch table Details section). parent directory must already exist; emit() auto-create directories. Tip: Use tempfile(fileext = ".md") inside vignettes examples example runs R CMD check without polluting package directory. format Explicit backend override. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. set, wins file extension. Useful writing .txt files contain RTF, round-trip testing, user custom backend registered non-standard name. data_file QC artefact writer. <character(1) | function(file) -> character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. set, writes resolved wide data frame alongside render. character path writes directly; lambda receives render path returns data file path (typical sponsor-flexible naming). Restriction: Returned-path extension must .csv, .tsv / .txt, .rds. Tip: data frame lambda governs pre-backend — CSV emitted regardless whether file RTF, PDF, DOCX.   manifest Emit CDISC ARS audit manifest sidecar. <logical(1)>: default FALSE. TRUE writes <file>.audit.yml verbatim CDISC ARS LDM v1.0 Output keys; see manifest = TRUE invariant Details section file contains determinism contract satisfies.
code: # Three canonical sponsor patterns for the lambda. data_file = \(f) paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f), "_qc.csv") data_file = \(f) file.path(   "validation",   paste0("val_", basename(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f)), ".csv") ) data_file = \(f) file.path(   "rd",   paste0("rd_", basename(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f)), ".rds") )

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: file path, invisibly. Use chaining emit() downstream consumer needs resolved path (e.g. printing link Quarto chunk, copying sidecar manifest archive, attaching render submission folder builder).

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
text: Validation /O. Every argument validated backend resolved engine runs. unsupported extension, malformed data_file path, missing backend raises tabular_error_input without writing file. spec resolves cleanly whose backend errors mid-write may leave partial file behind; failure mode touches disk. Backend dispatch. effective backend resolved file extension via table ; format argument always wins supplied. backend lives R/backend_<fmt>.R file self-registers package load time. Unknown extensions, missing extensions, formats registered backend raise tabular_error_input. error message lists currently registered formats failure actionable. data_file sponsor-neutral. Pass explicit path ("/qc.csv") fixed location, lambda (function(file) -> path) sponsor-flexible naming. lambda receives resolved render path can derive QC file (suffix, sibling folder, separate sponsor-styled name). Recognised extensions returned path .csv, .tsv (alias: .txt), .rds; anything else raises tabular_error_input. written data frame post- sort_rows() / post-engine_decimal() wide grid — exactly cell text backend wrote. manifest = TRUE writes sidecar. audit manifest written <file>.audit.yml next render (e.g. .md -> .audit.yml). Keys CDISC ARS LDM v1.0 Output verbatim: id, name, programmingCode (best-effort git + R + platform timestamp), fileSpecifications (sha256 every emitted artefact including data_file), displays/displaySections (Title / Header / Body / Footnote), referencedAnalyses (empty v0.1; reserved mintverse handoff), x-tabular (rendering geometry, pagination, style trace, input provenance). Determinism contract: two consecutive emit() calls byte- identical except rendered_at parameter timestamp; YAML round-trips yaml::read_yaml() + yaml::write_yaml(). Pure dispatcher. emit() rendering ; composes as_grid() backend writer. inspect resolved grid without writing file (development, build custom downstream consumer), call as_grid() directly.

title: Render a tabular_spec to a file — emit
code:
# ---- Example 1: Render demographics to Markdown ---- # # Smallest possible emit: spec in, .md out. The backend is chosen # from the file extension; the engine pipeline runs internally, # then the registered md backend writes a GFM pipe table you can # preview in any Markdown renderer. tempfile() keeps the example # clean for `R CMD check`. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short)  demo <- tabular(   saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"])   ),   footnotes = "Source: ADSL." ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo
N=%d",  n["placebo"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50
N=%d",  n["drug_50"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100
N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total
N=%d",    n["Total"]),    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label"))  demo_md <- tempfile(fileext = ".md") emit(demo, demo_md)  # ---- Example 2: Render + QC data + CDISC audit manifest ---- # # The clinical double-programming pattern: render the table, # write a QC CSV alongside it for an independent programmer to # verify cell-for-cell, and emit the CDISC ARS audit manifest # for submission packaging. The lambda derives the QC path from # the render path so the sponsor's naming convention lives in one # place. ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total))  ae_spec <- tabular(   ae,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term",     sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"])   ),   footnotes = "Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo
N=%d",  n["placebo"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50
N=%d",  n["drug_50"]),  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100
N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total
N=%d",    n["Total"]),    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE))  ae_md <- tempfile(fileext = ".md") emit(   ae_spec,   ae_md,   data_file = \(f) paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(f), "_qc.csv"),   manifest  = TRUE )  # ---- Example 3: Same spec, four backends — one-loop fan-out ---- # # `emit()` dispatches by file extension, so the same spec can # render to every backend in one loop. Useful for visual diffs # across formats during development and for shipping a build # artefact set (RTF for submission, HTML for review, PDF for the # CSR appendix). eff_spec <- tabular(eff_resp, titles = "Best Overall Response") |>   cols(     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Response"),     row_type   = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo    = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100   = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal")   )  out_dir <- tempfile() dir.create(out_dir) for (ext in c(".html", ".rtf", ".tex", ".docx", ".md")) {   emit(eff_spec, file.path(out_dir, paste0("eff", ext))) } list.files(out_dir) #> [1] "eff.docx" "eff.html" "eff.md"   "eff.rtf"  "eff.tex"   # ---- Example 4: QC artefact via data_file alongside the render ---- # # `emit(data_file = ...)` writes the resolved post-engine wide # data frame alongside the rendered table. The sponsor's QC # programmer picks up the side-car .csv (or .rds) and validates # cell values without parsing the rendered RTF. rtf_out  <- tempfile(fileext = ".rtf") data_out <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") emit(eff_spec, rtf_out, data_file = data_out) file.exists(rtf_out) #> [1] TRUE file.exists(data_out) #> [1] TRUE

title: Get the active session-default preset — get_preset

title: Get the active session-default preset — get_preset
code:
# ---- Example 1: Inspect after setting a session default ---- # # `get_preset()` returns NULL before any session default has been # attached, then returns the `preset_spec` after `set_preset()`. get_preset() # NULL #> NULL set_preset(font_size = 8, orientation = "landscape") active <- get_preset() is_preset_spec(active) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE active@font_size # 8 #> [1] 8 active@orientation # "landscape" #> [1] "landscape" # ---- Example 2: Copy the session default into a per-spec override ---- # # Read the session preset, tweak one knob for a single table, and # attach as a per-spec override without disturbing the session. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") # Read-tweak-attach without mutating the session default. base_knobs <- get_preset() tabular(saf_n) |> preset( font_size = base_knobs@font_size, paper_size = base_knobs@paper_size, orientation = "landscape" ) #> #>

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: Build column-header band(s) rendered table. named argument one band; value either character vector column names (leaf band) named list bands (inner band). Nesting depth arbitrary — engine renders one band row per depth level, cell spanning columns leaves.

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
code: headers(.spec, ...)

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: .spec tabular_spec attach header tree . <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind short user-supplied band label ... spec slot. ... Named header bands. name band label (must non-blank); value either: character vector data-column names — leaf band, named list whose entries follow recursive pattern — inner band. Inside nested-list value, unnamed character-vector entry declares passthrough leaf (see Passthrough section ). Restriction: Every column referenced must exist .spec@data. column may appear one leaf. Names must unique within one headers() call. Tip: Pass headers() arguments clear tree.

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining sort_rows(), style().

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: Replace, stack. second headers() call REPLACES prior tree — header structure single spec, stackable list. Call arguments clear tree. Strict label rule. Every declared band label must carry visible text — empty strings, NA, whitespace-labels rejected every nesting level. stricter col_spec(), accept empty labels (row-label column header text legitimate clinical case). silently-blank band layout artefact. Uncovered columns render naked. Columns referenced band render col_spec.label — extra band row . canonical pattern row-label columns (variable, soc, stat_label). Multi-line band labels. Embed \n band label two-line band cell (arm name row 1, BigN row 2).

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
text: Inside nested-list value, child entry may unnamed — entry character vector column names sit directly parent intermediate band depth. Use one column band sub-grouping siblings . strict-label rule still applies every declared band; unnamed passthrough band missing label — "band declared depth column."

title: Attach multi-level column headers — headers
code:
# ---- Example 1: Single "Treatment Group" band over four arms ---- # # AE-by-SOC/PT table with one flat band labelled "Treatment Group" # spanning the four arm columns and the Total column. The # row-label column (`soc`) sits to the left of the band with no # header covering it — the canonical clinical layout. ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( ae, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"])), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"])) ) |> headers( "Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total") ) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) #> #>

# ---- Example 2: Two-level nested band — Control vs Active arms ---- # # Efficacy BOR table where the active arms are grouped under an # "Active" sub-band and the placebo arm under a "Control" # sub-band, both under a single "Treatment Group" parent. # Demonstrates the named-list value form for arbitrary-depth # nesting. bor_levels <- c( "CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "Objective Response Rate (CR + PR)", "Disease Control Rate (CR + PR + SD)" ) eff <- eff_resp eff$stat_label <- factor(eff$stat_label, levels = bor_levels) ne <- stats::setNames(eff_n$n, eff_n$arm_short) tabular( eff, titles = c( "Table 14.2.1", "Best Overall Response and Response Rates", sprintf("Efficacy Evaluable Population (N=%d)", ne["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Response"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", ne["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", ne["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", ne["drug_100"])) ) |> headers( "Treatment Group" = list( "Control" = "placebo", "Active" = c("drug_50", "drug_100") ) ) |> sort_rows(by = "stat_label") #> #>

title: Mark a string as HTML for inline formatting — html
text: Wrap length-1 character vector tabular(), col_spec(), similar string slots interpret constrained HTML subset render time. Use CommonMark express formatting (custom CSS via <span style="...">, raw destination codes via <span data-rtf="...">).

title: Mark a string as HTML for inline formatting — html
code: html(text)

title: Mark a string as HTML for inline formatting — html
text: text HTML fragment. <character(1)>: required. Length-1 character vector. NA rejected.

title: Mark a string as HTML for inline formatting — html
text: length-1 character vector classed c("from_html", "character"). Pass directly string-bearing slot (tabular() titles / footnotes, col_spec() label, style() pretext / posttext); resolve engine calls parse_inline() internally backends walk resulting inline_ast.

title: Mark a string as HTML for inline formatting — html
text:
Recognised tag whitelist.

,
/
, , , , <>, , , , , . Tags outside set drop wrapper keep text content (arbitrary HTML attack surface). Span styles. x parses style attribute named character vector (c(color = "red", "font-weight" = "bold")). Backends translate CSS keys destination-specific markup (RTF \cf, LaTeX \textcolor, DOCX , HTML inline style). Backend-specific raw codes. span data-rtf, data-latex, data-html, data-docx attributes carries per-backend raw markup. matching backend emits data value verbatim ignores others; non-matching backends render span's text content plain. Use cases AST express portably.

what: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: Wrap length-1 character vector tabular(), col_spec(), style() pretext / posttext, similar string slots interpret CommonMark Markdown render time. Supports GitHub-flavoured plus Pandoc-style superscript (^sup^) subscript (~sub~) extensions; raw HTML inside Markdown passes constrained tag set documented html().

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
code: md(text)

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: text Markdown string. <character(1)>: required. Length-1 character vector. NA rejected; empty string "" renders content.

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: length-1 character vector classed c("from_markdown", "character"). Pass directly string-bearing slot (tabular() titles / footnotes, col_spec() label, style() pretext / posttext); resolve engine calls parse_inline() internally backends walk resulting inline_ast.

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
text: Convention adopted gt. Marking strings md() html() mirrors well-tested gt convention. Plain (unwrapped) strings render plain text — stray ** silently bold surrounding span. Wrap explicitly opt . Recognised Markdown. **bold**, *italic*, `code`, [link text](url), hard line break (two trailing spaces + \ + ), Pandoc ^sup^ ~sub~. Single embedded ("soft break" CommonMark) renders space HTML; tabular preserves line break clinical-table use multi-line cells / titles routine. HTML pass-. Raw HTML Markdown (e.g. md("Drug warning")) parsed HTML using tag whitelist html(). Tags outside whitelist drop wrapper keep text content. Composition plain strings. md() html() wrap input internal control-character prefix survives c() concatenation, can freely mix plain marked strings single character vector: c("Table 14.3.1", md("**Drug **"), "third"). Backends strip marker rendering; users never see .

title: Mark a string as Markdown for inline formatting — md
code:
# ---- Example 1: Bold title with Pandoc-style footnote marker ---- # # AE-by-SOC/PT table. The third title line bolds "Safety # Population (N=86)" via `md("**...**")`; the first footnote # carries a Pandoc-style superscript marker `^a^` that the # backends render as a true superscript. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( saf_aesocpt, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term", md(sprintf("**Safety Population (N=%d)**", n["Total"])) ), footnotes = c( md("^a^ Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT.") ) ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total\nN=%d", n["Total"])) ) #> #>

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: Attach pagination_spec tabular_spec. engine uses spec render time decide page breaks fall, wide tables split horizontal panels, continuation marker () prints continued pages. row budget per page computed engine active preset (paper, orientation, margins, font size) chrome rows consumed titles, column headers, footnotes — set rows-per-page directly.

title: Configure pagination — paginate
code: paginate(   spec,   keep_together = character(),   panels = 1,   orphan_floor = 3,   widow_floor = 2,   repeat_content = c("titles", "headers", "footnotes"),   continuation = NULL )

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: spec tabular_spec attach pagination . <tabular_spec>: required. keep_together Group columns whose runs identical values must split across page break. <character>: default character(). Every entry must usage = "group" column declared cols(). Interaction: run tall fit computed row budget less orphan_floor split anyway; pagination best-effort, hard contract.   panels Number horizontal panels wide tables. <integer(1) | "auto">: default 1. 1, every column every page (single vertical scroll). N > 1, engine splits non-group columns N chunks repeats every group column every panel. Note: "auto" accepted treated 1 preset-aware column-width metrics land; , "auto" split total table width exceeds printable area. orphan_floor Minimum rows continued-page. <integer(1)>: default 3. keep_together move page break back far fewer orphan_floor rows ride current page, engine splits protected run anyway. Acts escape valve groups tall fit. widow_floor Minimum rows final page. <integer(1)>: default 2. last page carry fewer widow_floor rows, engine merges rows back onto previous page (page overflow accepted). Avoids "one-row-orphaned--page-N" look without complicating primary split rule. repeat_content page chrome repeats every page. <character>: default c("titles", "headers", "footnotes"). subset three values; governed independently: "titles" — title block every page (else page 1 ). "headers" — column-header band every page (else page 1 ). "footnotes" — footnote block every page (else last page ). default repeats three page self-contained per submission layout contract. Pass subset drop one (e.g. c("headers", "footnotes") keeps title page 1 ), character() repeat nothing. Note: Footnotes always anchored page foot present; membership chooses every-page vs last-page-, never table-body placement. HTML / MD: ignored. HTML renders one continuous <table> browsers natively repeat <thead> print; MD print model. Effective page-oriented backends (RTF, PDF, LaTeX, DOCX). continuation Marker text appended continuing table's title block. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. NULL (default) renders marker — pick wording submission style guide expects (e.g. "(continued)", "(Cont'd)", "Page %d %d") pass explicitly. HTML / MD: ignored. one continuous document screen continuing-page boundary mark. Effective page-oriented backends (RTF, PDF, LaTeX, DOCX).
code: # Protect the SOC-level grouping in an AE-by-SOC/PT table. paginate(keep_together = "soc")

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining style(), preset(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Configure pagination — paginate
text: Replace, stack. second paginate() call REPLACES prior spec — pagination single configuration block, stackable list. Call defaults clear back engine's auto behaviour. Rows per page computed, configured. engine takes paper height active orientation (letter, a4) subtracts top + bottom margins, title block height (number title lines + blank separator), column-header band height (max embedded \n line count across visible column labels, plus spanning header levels), footnote block height (number footnote lines + blank separator). remainder, divided row height active font size, gives body-row budget per page. Landscape pages naturally carry fewer rows portrait paper size; smaller fonts carry . keep_together protects group runs. page break fall middle contiguous run identical values usage = "group" column listed keep_together, engine moves break BACK start run whole run rides next page. Single rule escape: moving break back leave fewer orphan_floor rows current page, engine splits run anyway (single group tall fit one page kept together). panels group stickiness. panels > 1, engine splits NON-group columns approximately equal slices repeats every usage = "group" column every panel row context. panels = "auto" defers decision preset-aware column-width metrics; metrics land future release engine treats "auto" 1.

title: Configure pagination — paginate
code:
# ---- Example 1: AE table paginated by SOC ---- # # AE-by-SOC/PT table that may run several pages. The SOC column is # protected by `keep_together` so a page break never lands in the # middle of one SOC's PT rows. The engine derives the row budget # from the preset's orientation + font_size + paper size and from # the title / footnote / header line counts on the spec — no # manual rows-per-page knob to keep in sync. ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( ae, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(usage = "group", visible = FALSE, group_display = "column_repeat"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"])), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"])) ) |> headers("Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) |> paginate( keep_together = "soc", repeat_content = c("titles", "headers", "footnotes"), continuation = "(continued)" ) #> #>

# ---- Example 2: Wide ACROSS-style efficacy table split across 2 panels ---- # # BOR table where the four-arm column block is too wide for portrait # paper. Split into 2 horizontal panels; the group column # (`stat_label`) repeats on every panel for row context. Vertical # pagination still applies, so on a tall table you would see panel A # pages 1-2, then panel B pages 1-2. bor_levels <- c( "CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "Objective Response Rate (CR + PR)", "Disease Control Rate (CR + PR + SD)" ) eff <- eff_resp eff$stat_label <- factor(eff$stat_label, levels = bor_levels) ne <- stats::setNames(eff_n$n, eff_n$arm_short) tabular( eff, titles = c( "Table 14.2.1", "Best Overall Response and Response Rates", sprintf("Efficacy Evaluable Population (N=%d)", ne["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Response"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", ne["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", ne["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", ne["drug_100"])) ) |> sort_rows(by = "stat_label") |> paginate(panels = 2, repeat_content = c("titles", "headers", "footnotes")) #> #>

what: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: pivot_across() tabular's input-side helper: consumes long Analysis Results Data (ARD) data frame (typically produced cards::ard_stack() cards::ard_stack_hierarchical()) returns wide display data.frame ready pass tabular().

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
code: pivot_across(   data,   statistic = list(continuous = "{mean} ({sd})", categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"),   column = NULL,   label = NULL,   overall = "Total",   decimals = NULL,   fmt = NULL )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: data Long ARD input data. <data.frame>: required. minimum needs stat_name stat. Cards-style group columns (group1, group1_level, ...) variable / variable_level auto-detected. Tibbles / card objects / arrow tables coerced via .data.frame(). statistic Format spec cell composition. <character(1) | named list>: required. Combines one ARD stats one display cell. Three accepted forms — illustrated . Inside format string, {stat_name} substitutes stat's value ARD (example, "{n} ({p}%)" interpolates n p stats "53 (62%)" cell). lookup order value needed variable : per-variable -> per-context -> default -> literal "{n}". column Grouping column whose unique values become arms. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. NULL auto-detects ARD's group1 value — renamed input — picks single non-standard column. Pass string multiple group columns exist. label Variable-name display-label map. <character> | NULL: default NULL. Named character vector mapping variable names display labels (e.g. c(AGE = "Age (years)", SEX = "Sex")). Applies variable, soc, label columns output. NULL leaves upstream variable names verbatim. overall Column name NA-arm (overall / total) rows. <character(1) | NULL>: default "Total". Pass NULL drop overall rows entirely (per-arm output). decimals Per-stat decimal precision. <named integer | named list>: default c()“. Accepts two forms: named integer vector — global per-stat overrides (c(mean = 1, sd = 2, p = 0)). named list — per-variable plus .default (list(AGE = c(mean = 2), .default = c(p = 1))). Built-defaults apply neither sets stat. fmt Per-stat custom formatter functions. <named list function>: default list()“. function takes numeric value returns character string; overrides built-ins decimals stat. Useful p-value styling domain-specific formatting.
code: # p-value formatter: render below-threshold values as "<0.001". fmt = list(   p.value = function(x) {     ifelse(x < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", x))   } )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: One format string applied every variable regardless context. Use ARD homogeneous (e.g. categorical).
code: # Every variable rendered as "n (p%)" — categorical-only slice. cat_only <- saf_demo_card[saf_demo_card$context == "categorical", ] pivot_across(   cat_only,   statistic = "{n} ({p}%)" )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: Different formats continuous vs categorical contexts. typical clinical-table form demographics mix two.
code: # AGE (continuous) -> "75.2 (8.59)"; SEX (categorical) -> "53 (62%)" pivot_across(   saf_demo_card,   statistic = list(     continuous  = "{mean} ({sd})",     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: Override per-variable basis; fall back default context. Use one variable needs custom format.
code: # AGE shows just the mean; SEX / RACE keep the categorical default. pivot_across(   saf_demo_card,   statistic = list(     AGE         = "{mean}",     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)",     default     = "{mean} ({sd})"   ) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: single entry can named character vector — element becomes one display row, name row label. Use N / Mean (SD) / Median / Min, Max-style blocks.
code: pivot_across(   saf_demo_card,   statistic = list(     continuous = c(       N           = "{N}",       "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})",       Median      = "{median}",       "Min, Max"  = "{min}, {max}"     ),     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: wide data.frame ready tabular(). Schema: variable — variable name (label label = ...). stat_label — display-row label. One column per arm level (named group1_level values renamed arm column). Total (whatever overall set ) applicable. Hierarchical ARD adds soc, label, row_type instead variable. Pass result straight tabular() start render pipeline.

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: tabular's package boundary display-: pre-summarised data , rendered file . pivot_across() canonical bridge cards aggregation backend boundary. aggregate — pivots arms columns, interpolates per-cell display strings stat values, applies decimal precision. Filtering, weighting, aggregation happen upstream cards data-prep step.

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: row whose n value equals zero renders whole cell bare n value instead fully interpolating format string. categorical level n = 0, cell shows "0", "0 (0.0%)". clinical convention — empty cells read single zero, advertise meaningless rate. default fires (chain events). cell assembly, format-string interpolation, engine checks row's n stat. zero, engine short-circuits returns formatted n value ("0") entire cell — {p} never substituted, (0.0%) half format string dropped. opt : supply custom fmt$n. Setting function fmt$n engine's signal user owns n rendering. short-circuit disabled whole table; every row full format string interpolates, {n} becomes formatter's output {p} becomes standard percentage. n = 0, "0 (0.0%)".
code: # Force "0 (0.0%)" for n = 0 rows by attaching a custom n formatter. # The body of fmt$n can be the default integer rendering — its # presence alone is what disables the zero-suppression branch. pivot_across(   saf_demo_card,   statistic = list(     continuous  = "{mean} ({sd})",     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ),   fmt = list(n = function(x) sprintf("%d", as.integer(x))) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
text: percentage otherwise round 0 (value positive smaller chosen precision) renders <0.1; one round 100 (positive smaller 100) renders >99.9. threshold precision-aware: decimals = c(p = 2) produces <0.01 / >99.99. matches pharma convention never claiming exactly 0% 100% least one subject contributed. Override per-stat via fmt:   fmt$p receives raw stat value (proportion 0 1) returns displayed string. pharma-threshold branch fires inside built-p formatter decimals-driven path, custom fmt$p bypasses .
code: # Show exact rounded percentages even at the extremes pivot_across(   data,   statistic = "{n} ({p}%)",   decimals  = c(p = 1),   fmt = list(p = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x * 100)) )

title: Convert a cards ARD to a wide display data.frame — pivot_across
code:
# ---- Example 1: Demographics — long ARD to rendered spec ---- # # Full pipeline from a `cards::ard_stack()`-style long ARD to a # sorted `tabular_spec`. The multi-row continuous block (N / # Mean (SD) / Median / Min, Max) sits above each categorical # block; decimals are set per-stat (mean 1, sd 2, p 1) to match # the CDISC convention. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) saf_demo_card |> pivot_across( statistic = list( continuous = c( N = "{N}", "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})", Median = "{median}", "Min, Max" = "{min}, {max}" ), categorical = "{n} ({p}%)" ), decimals = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, p = 1, median = 1, min = 0, max = 0), label = c(AGE = "Age (years)", SEX = "Sex", RACE = "Race") ) |> tabular( titles = c( "Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Percentages based on N per treatment group." ) |> cols( variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"), stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"), Placebo = col_spec( label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal" ), `Xanomeline Low Dose` = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal" ), `Xanomeline High Dose` = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal" ), Total = col_spec( label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"]), align = "decimal" ) ) #> #>

#>
# ---- Example 2: Hierarchical SOC/PT AE table ---- # # Hierarchical `cards::ard_stack_hierarchical()` output threaded # through `pivot_across()`. The hierarchical ARD emits a # (soc, label, row_type) triple plus one stat row per (arm, SOC, PT); # `pivot_across()` folds the arm dimension to columns and preserves # the hierarchy markers. Derive `indent_level` from `row_type` so # `col_spec(indent_by = "indent_level")` drives the SOC -> PT # indent on the `label` column. wide <- saf_aesocpt_card |> pivot_across(statistic = "{n} ({p}%)") wide$indent_level <- as.integer(wide$row_type == "pt") tabular( wide, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = c( "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT.", "Percentages based on N per treatment group." ) ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), Placebo = col_spec( label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal" ), `Xanomeline Low Dose` = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal" ), `Xanomeline High Dose` = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal" ) ) #> #>

what: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: Attach preset_spec tabular_spec, carrying page-geometry knobs (paper, orientation, margins, body font_size + family, h-rule policy, decimal metric, typography defaults). engine consults per-spec preset first computing per-page row budget, decimal-aligned column widths, chrome backend renders around body grid.

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
code: preset(.spec, ..., .template = NULL, .style = NULL, .reset = FALSE)

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: .spec tabular_spec attach preset . <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind knob name ... spec slot. ... Named preset knobs. subset 13 knobs preset_spec class carries. Knob values validated class's enum / length / type rules; bad values raise tabular_error_input. Unknown knob names raise tabular_error_input recognised set listed. Recognised knobs: font_size — body point size. <numeric(1)>. font_family — body font family. <character | character(1)>. Default "mono". Three accepted shapes: Generic family — "mono" (default), "serif", "sans" (CSS aliases "monospace" / "sans-serif" also recognised). resolver expands per-backend chain leads Linux-installed Liberation face (Posit Workbench / Domino / Citrix / RStudio Server), Microsoft Office face (Courier New / Times New Roman / Arial) desktop Win / Mac consumers, TeX Gyre LaTeX compile, CSS generic HTML. Liberation Mono / Serif / Sans metric-compatible Courier New / TNR / Arial, layout, line breaks, decimal alignment hold across every render context. mono default matches dominant submission-TFL convention deterministic glyph widths drive n (%) cell alignment. Named alias — "Times", "Times New Roman", "Arial", "Helvetica", "Courier", "Courier New". PostScript-era names alias appropriate generic family (Times -> serif, Arial / Helvetica -> sans, Courier -> mono) emit expanded chain. Honours user's intent ("want Times-like rendering") every OS instead hard-erroring Linux server TNR installed. Named font — "Inter", "JetBrains Mono", "Source Serif Pro", sponsor-specific face, etc. Emitted verbatim fallback fabricated. consuming app (browser, xelatex, Word, LibreOffice) resolves name font matcher. RTF DOCX fall back consuming app's substitution table name missing; xelatex hard-errors compile time; HTML browsers fall browser's default font (necessarily class-matched). Explicit stack — c("Inter", "Helvetica", "sans"). User owns chain. Returned verbatim — alias lookup bypassed, c("Times", "Times") honours exact name chain expansion (escape hatch users genuinely want exact-name semantics). Note: Adobe Source Pro longer default lead. Source Pro pre-installed production Linux servers, leading walks 2-3 missing names resolving. Users installed Source Pro can opt via explicit-stack form (c("Source Serif Pro", "serif")). see Word's font dropdown vs. renders. open tabular-generated .rtf Word macOS Windows, font dropdown displays file's requested face — "Liberation Mono" default (Linux-server-installed face). rendered text screen whatever Word's \\*\\falt substitution resolved — typically Courier New macOS / Windows. correct: Liberation Mono Courier New metric-compatible design, rendered layout (line breaks, decimal alignment, page breaks) identical regardless face Word actually used render. \\*\\falt substitution model applies serif (Liberation Serif -> Times New Roman) sans (Liberation Sans -> Arial). force Office names primary. reviewers confused seeing "Liberation Mono" Word font dropdown (cosmetic concern; affect rendering), pass explicit length>1 stack Office name first. resolver returns vector verbatim — alias lookup, chain expansion — RTF file names Office face primary chosen alternate \\*\\falt:   canonical escape hatch authors know consumer audience Mac / Windows Word users want dropdown show Office face directly. orientation — page orientation. <character(1)>. One "landscape" (default), "portrait". paper_size — paper key. <character(1)>. One "letter" (default), "a4". margins — page margins inches. <numeric(1) | numeric(4)>. Length 1 = four sides; length 4 = top, right, bottom, left. pagehead, pagefoot — per-page header / footer band content. <list>. band named list slots left / center / right; every slot name rejected. slot accepts NULL (omit), character scalar, character vector (multi-row content), inline_ast. Empty list() (default) -> band emitted. Single-row form (scalar slots):   Multi-row form (vector slots, index-aligned):   Growth direction. Vector index 1 = body edge; index N = far body. pagehead rows stack upward away table (row closest table index 1). pagefoot rows stack downward away table (row closest table index 1). Shorter slots pad "" FAR end (high index), scalar slot naturally lands body-edge row. Token vocabulary — substituted slot text: {program}, {program_path}, {datetime} resolve per render (as_grid() / emit()); {page} {npages} resolve per page (filled Word / xelatex / browser's print engine view time). program tokens walk 5-mode detection chain — RStudio API, source() frame, Rscript / R CMD BATCH commandArgs (covers Domino + Linux batch + CI), knitr current_input, fallback "<interactive>". rules — single border vocabulary (replaces old borders knob). String sugar "booktabs" (default, clinical baseline), "grid", "frame", "none"; single brdr() broadcast every active rule; named list keyed nine rule names (toprule, midrule, bottomrule, spanrule, rowrule, footnoterule, leftrule, rightrule, colrule) — unlisted rules keep default, bare string "none" drops one. rules = list(rowrule = brdr()) reproduces old hlines = "". bottomrule vs footnoterule. mutually exclusive: exactly one rule sits data -> footnote boundary. default bottomrule (table's bottom edge); footnoterule (table-width rule opening footnote section) default. distinct footnote-section rule, footnoterule drawn paginated backends — RTF, LaTeX / PDF, DOCX. HTML backend continuous (non-paginated) separate footnote section, folds one rule: whichever bottomrule / footnoterule active becomes table's bottom edge (bottomrule wins set). Setting footnoterule therefore still draws closing rule HTML — rendered bottomrule. spacing — region-keyed blank-line control. named list keyed title / body / subgroup / footnote, named numeric c(= , = ) (footnote: ). Default Appendix-one blank line title block. Two adjoining region-sides target physical gap resolve MAX (never sum), gap never accidentally doubled. stripe — zebra body-row fills. single colour (applied even rows) named c(odd = , even = ); NULL (default) . indent_size — row-label indent width, monospace- space units. <integer(1)>. Default 2L. indent level adds many space-widths left padding cell. 0L disables indent prefix entirely. Block alignment title / footnote / header / subgroup / body surfaces set via alignment named-list knob (alignment = list(title_halign = "left", ...)), scalar knob; blank-line spacing set via spacing (). na_text — global NA fallback. <character(1)>. decimal_metrics — decimal-padding metric. <character(1)>. title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: Per-spec, chained. preset() per-spec override — verb returns modified spec, composable pipe alongside cols() / headers() / paginate(). Use single table needs one-geometry (e.g. landscape A4 one wide efficacy summary inside portfolio portrait letter tables). Merge, replace. second preset() call merges scalar knobs onto spec's existing preset; unspecified knobs keep prior value. five named-list knobs (alignment / rules / fonts / colors / padding) lower style_layer records preset@style via .preset_args_to_layers() (internal) append call order; layer order precedence within engine cascade, later preset() call's lowered attribute wins earlier one cell. Pass .reset = TRUE discard existing knobs start preset_spec() defaults. preset(.spec, .reset = TRUE) knobs clears per-spec override entirely (spec falls set_preset() preset_spec() defaults render time). Direct preset_spec() calls bypass lowering. five named-list knobs longer slots preset_spec S7 class — exist preset() / set_preset() arguments lower @style. preset_spec(rules = list(...)) (analogous direct calls) raise "unused argument". Wrap calls tabular(...) |> preset(...) lowering helper fires layers land @style. Cascade set_preset(). engine resolves active preset order: (1) spec's per-call preset (verb), (2) session default attached via set_preset(), (3) preset_spec() factory defaults. first non-NULL layer wins; layers field-merged across cascade.

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset

# ---- Example 1: Landscape A4 for a wide efficacy table ---- # # BOR table where the four-arm column block fits portrait letter # with a smaller body font, but the sponsor wants A4 landscape at # 8pt for visual breathing room. `preset()` attaches the geometry; # `paginate()` reads it later to size the per-page row budget. bor_levels <- c( "CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "Objective Response Rate (CR + PR)", "Disease Control Rate (CR + PR + SD)" ) eff <- eff_resp eff$stat_label <- factor(eff$stat_label, levels = bor_levels) ne <- stats::setNames(eff_n$n, eff_n$arm_short) tabular( eff, titles = c( "Table 14.2.1", "Best Overall Response and Response Rates", sprintf("Efficacy Evaluable Population (N=%d)", ne["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Response"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", ne["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", ne["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", ne["drug_100"])) ) |> sort_rows(by = "stat_label") |> preset( orientation = "landscape", paper_size = "a4", font_size = 8 ) |> paginate() #> #>

#>
# ---- Example 2: Per-spec override with per-page chrome ---- # # The submission session sets a portrait letter 9pt default (typical # safety-table geometry). One particular AE table needs landscape # for a long PT label band; the per-spec `preset()` overrides only # orientation. The same per-spec call wires the canonical # per-page header band (protocol on the left, page X of Y on the # right) and a footer band that auto-resolves the calling # script's name and the current render timestamp via the # `{program}` and `{datetime}` tokens. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( ae, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(usage = "group", visible = FALSE, group_display = "column_repeat"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"])), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"])) ) |> headers("Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) |> preset( orientation = "landscape", pagehead = list( left = "Protocol: ABC-123", right = "Page {page} of {npages}" ), pagefoot = list( left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}" ) ) |> paginate(keep_together = "soc") #> #>

title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: .spec tabular_spec apply minimal theme . <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed partial matching bind ... knob spec slot. ... Named preset knobs. title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining paginate() / style(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: sets, theme (lowest) precedence explicit later style() wins: Rules. Drops booktabs toprule bottomrule (outer frame), keeping midrule column labels muted column-spanner spanrule. Equivalent preset(rules = list(toprule = "none", bottomrule = "none")). Weight. Sets bold = FALSE title, column-header, subgroup-label, group-header surfaces. HTML backend overrides font-weight: 600 class default inline font-weight: normal; paginated backends (RTF / LaTeX / PDF / DOCX) suppress surface's bold run. Last verb wins. weight layers ride theme tier, later explicit style(bold = TRUE, .= cells_title()) (surface) re-bolds . Treat preset_minimal() theme baseline override individual surfaces afterwards. Markdown. GFM represent colour / background / font surface; rendering styled surface .md emits one-time tabular_warning_fidelity degrades gracefully. Weight (bold) italic carry .

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Renders tabular_spec interactively. default behaviour mirrors gt::gt(): convert spec htmltools tag list let htmltools dispatch — RStudio + Positron viewer panes, Quarto / Rmd notebook inline, Databricks displayHTML, plain-console cat() handled without IDE- specific branching.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: x tabular_spec render. <tabular_spec>: required. object hand emit(). ... Forwarded htmltools::print / .tags(). Use pass id, style, class overrides wrapping <div>. view Open viewer? <logical(1)>: default interactive()“. role gt::gt's `view` argument: passes htmltools `browse = view`. Set `view = FALSE` suppress viewer one call (e.g. capture HTML string without launching window). output Force specific preview format. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL (auto). See output argument section full list. session default can set via options(tabular_print_output = "cli") users prefer structural summary HTML preview.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Invisibly returns x. Side effect: opens viewer, inlines chunk, cat()s output.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Dispatch. print() delegates .tags.tabular_spec() returns htmltools::tagList. tag list handed htmltools's print method browse = view: htmltools opens IDE viewer one registered, inlines Quarto / Rmd chunk running inside one, cat()s HTML neither applies. is_rstudio() / is_positron() / is_notebook() heuristics — htmltools already knows. view argument. Defaults interactive(), universal -switch gt::gt() uses. Non-interactive contexts (Rscript, R CMD check, CI, devtools::test) bypass viewer automatically. Pass view = FALSE explicitly interactive prompt suppress viewer single call. output argument. Forces specific preview format instead default HTML-via-htmltools path. One : "html" — default, explicit. "md" / "markdown" — cat() markdown source console (round-trips backend_md). "latex" — cat() markdown source temporary placeholder (real LaTeX preview lands backend_latex). "rtf" / "docx" / "pdf" — render HTML preview emit cli note pointing emit() real artefact. viewer pane render RTF / OOXML, deliberately compile tinytex every autoprint. "cli" — print structural cli-tree summary (props, headers, sort, pagination, preset). Useful debugging spec composition without paying HTML render cost. Robustness. HTML render wrapped tryCatch; rendering fails reason printer falls back cli-tree summary cli::cli_warn() describing failure, broken spec never crashes REPL. Tempdir. Preview HTML files live getOption("tabular_preview_dir", default = tempdir()). Override option keep stable location (handy Linux distros browsers read access /tmp/).

text: Pre-summarised AE--SOC/PT table. Interleaved row order: overall "TEAE" row first, per-SOC blocks SOC row followed preferred-term detail rows. Top 10 SOCs top 5 PTs per SOC kept; row_type marks role row indent_level carries canonical depth (0 overall SOC, 1 PT) downstream pipeline drives SOC -> PT indent via col_spec(indent_by = "indent_level") without reconstructing every script. richer SOC × PT slice exercises paginate() engine's horizontal-panel splitter end--end realistic submission shell.

title: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — saf_aesocpt
text: data frame 61 rows 10 columns: soc System Organ Class label. Repeats across SOC's PT rows; hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE) label carries SOC text SOC rows. label row's display label. Equal soc overall SOC-summary rows; equal preferred-term name PT detail rows. Promoted primary display column — pair indent_by = "indent_level" drive SOC -> PT indent. row_type One "overall", "soc", "pt". Partition marker; hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE). indent_level Integer depth (0 overall SOC rows, 1 PT rows). Consumed col_spec(indent_by = "indent_level") label column; engine auto-hides column resolve time. n_total Integer. row's subject count — overall TEAE count overall row, SOC's count SOC row, PT's count PT row. Inner sort key. soc_n Integer. parent SOC's count, broadcast every row SOC's cluster (SOC row + PT children) descending sort soc_n keeps PTs grouped parent. overall row, equal overall TEAE count. Outer sort key. placebo Placebo arm cell text ("n (pct)"). drug_50, drug_100 Drug arms cell text. Total Pooled-across-arms cell text.

title: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — saf_aesocpt
code:
# 95% safety pattern: SOC/PT table where `label` carries SOC text # on SOC rows and PT text on PT rows, indented by `indent_level`. # `soc` / `row_type` / `n_total` / `soc_n` ride along as hidden # partition + sort keys. `sort_rows(soc_n, n_total)` clusters PTs # under their parent SOC and orders both levels by descending count. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( saf_aesocpt, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ) ) |> cols( label = col_spec( label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level", align = "left" ), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), soc_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec( label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal" ), drug_50 = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal" ), drug_100 = col_spec( label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal" ), Total = col_spec( label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"]), align = "decimal" ) ) |> sort_rows( by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE) ) #> #>
#>

text: data frame 35 rows 6 columns: variable Display-block label ("Age (years)", "Age Group, n (%)", "Sex, n (%)", "Race, n (%)", "Ethnicity, n (%)"). Driven cols(usage = "group") collapse repeat values render. stat_label Statistic level label ("n", "Mean (SD)", "Median", "M", "WHITE", ...). placebo Placebo arm cell text. drug_50 Xanomeline Low Dose (50 mg) arm cell text. drug_100 Xanomeline High Dose (100 mg) arm cell text. Total Pooled-across-arms cell text.

text: demographics summary saf_demo, long Analysis Results Data (ARD) format produced cards::ard_stack(). One row per (treatment arm, variable, statistic). Shipped teaching dataset shows upstream shape users typically start cards. Convert wide form tabular() accepts via pivot_across() — tabular consume long ARD format, since pre-summarised wide data package boundary.

title: Cards ARD for demographics (flat ARD companion) — saf_demo_card
text: Continuous variables: AGE, WEIGHT, HEIGHT, BMI (emitting N, mean, sd, median, p25, p75, min, max). Categorical variables: AGEGR1, SEX, RACE, ETHNIC, BMI_CAT (emitting n, N, p). package's canonical flat ARD demo. hierarchical counterpart saf_aesocpt_card; together cover shapes pivot_across() must handle.

title: Vital-signs summary — saf_vital

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
code:
# ---- Example 1: Sticky session default for an analysis script ---- # # The submission's safety tables all use portrait letter, 9pt # Times New Roman with 1-inch margins. Set once at the top of the # analysis script and every `tabular()` chain inherits it — no # per-table `preset()` call needed unless one table deviates. set_preset( font_size = 9, font_family = "Times New Roman", orientation = "portrait", paper_size = "letter", margins = 1 ) # Subsequent tabular() chains pick up the session preset at render. demo_n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( saf_aeoverall, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Overall Summary of Adverse Events", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", demo_n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Subjects counted once per category." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Category"), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", demo_n["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", demo_n["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", demo_n["drug_100"])), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", demo_n["Total"])) ) #> #>
#>

#>
# ---- Example 2: Reset the session default mid-script ---- # # The first half of the script produces safety tables at 9pt; the # second half produces efficacy tables at 10pt on landscape A4. A # single `set_preset(.reset = TRUE, ...)` resets the cascade before # the second batch starts. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") get_preset()@font_size # 9 #> [1] 9 set_preset( .reset = TRUE, font_size = 10, orientation = "landscape", paper_size = "a4" ) get_preset()@orientation # "landscape" #> [1] "landscape" # Reset the session default so subsequent examples / R sessions # are not affected. set_preset(.reset = TRUE) # ---- Example 3: Save and restore around a renegade table ---- # # Most of the submission renders portrait letter at 9pt. One # renegade efficacy table needs landscape A4 at 10pt. Capture # the prior session preset, render the renegade, then restore. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") old <- set_preset( font_size = 10, paper_size = "a4", orientation = "landscape" ) # ... one renegade render ... if (is.null(old)) { set_preset(.reset = TRUE) # was no prior — clear } else { set_preset(old) # round-trip via the positional `new` arg } get_preset()@paper_size # "letter" — restored #> [1] "letter" # ---- Example 4: Snapshot current preset, mutate, restore ---- # # Capture whatever the session preset is right now (may be NULL), # let a downstream helper mutate it, then put it back when done. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") snapshot <- get_preset() # Simulate downstream code mutating session state. set_preset(font_size = 11, orientation = "landscape") # Restore. The wholesale-install path of `set_preset(new)` # accepts any `preset_spec` returned by `get_preset()` / # `set_preset()`. if (is.null(snapshot)) { set_preset(.reset = TRUE) } else { set_preset(snapshot) } get_preset()@font_size # 9 — restored #> [1] 9 # Reset for subsequent examples / R sessions. set_preset(.reset = TRUE)

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: Attach sort_spec tabular_spec. engine applies sort pagination, may reference column spec@data whether column declared cols().

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
code: sort_rows(.spec, by = character(), descending = FALSE)

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: .spec tabular_spec attach sort . <tabular_spec>: required. Ordered column names sort , precedence order. <character>: default character(). Length 0 accepted (-op sort). May reference columns declared cols() — sort-helper columns ride along engine. Restriction: Every entry must column spec@data. reference arm columns produced pivot_across(); pivot upstream sort instead. Arm cells hold rendered stat strings (e.g. "75.2 (8.3)") order meaningfully.   descending Per-key sort direction. <logical(1) | logical(length())>: default FALSE. TRUE sorts corresponding key descending; length 1 recycles every key. Restriction: NAs. Length must 1 length(). Tip: mixed-direction multi-key sorts, pass length() values; engine inverts xtfrm rank descending key calls order() keys.
code: # Two-key clinical sort: row_type ascending, n_total descending. sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE))

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining style(), paginate(), preset(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: Replace, stack. second sort_rows() call REPLACES prior sort — sort single spec, stackable list. Call arguments clear. NA last, regardless direction. NA values sort key placed end whether key ascending descending (matching order(..., na.last = TRUE)). Factor levels drive order. Factor columns sort factor levels, character label. CDISC BOR ordering (CR < PR < SD < NON-CR/NON-PD < PD < NE < MISSING) survives tabular pipeline without explicit mutate() — coerce stat_label factor levels clinical order upstream, sort_rows(= "stat_label") rest.

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
code:
# ---- Example 1: AE table sorted by SOC, then by descending subject count ---- # # AE-by-SOC/PT table where the SOCs and PTs appear in descending # order of subject count within the row-type hierarchy (overall # first, then SOCs, then PTs). `saf_aesocpt$Total` cells are # formatted text ("171 (67.3)"), so a lexical sort on `Total` # would be wrong ("14" < "171" < "29") — attach a numeric rank # column upstream and sort on (row_type, n_total). ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( ae, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"])), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"])) ) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) #> #>
#>

#>
# ---- Example 2: BOR table in CDISC factor order ---- # # Efficacy BOR table that must appear in CDISC clinical order # (CR < PR < SD < NON-CR/NON-PD < PD < NE < MISSING < ORR < DCR), # not alphabetical. `eff_resp$stat_label` arrives as character, so # coerce to a factor with the canonical levels upstream and # `sort_rows()` uses those levels directly. bor_levels <- c( "CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "Objective Response Rate (CR + PR)", "Disease Control Rate (CR + PR + SD)" ) eff <- eff_resp eff$stat_label <- factor(eff$stat_label, levels = bor_levels) ne <- stats::setNames(eff_n$n, eff_n$arm_short) tabular( eff, titles = c( "Table 14.2.1", "Best Overall Response and Response Rates", sprintf("Efficacy Evaluable Population (N=%d)", ne["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Response"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", ne["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", ne["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", ne["drug_100"])) ) |> sort_rows(by = "stat_label") #> #>

# ---- Example 3: Mixed-direction multi-key sort with hidden helper ---- # # Demographics-style table sorted by `variable` ascending and a # hidden numeric key descending. The `descending` argument takes # one value per `by` entry so each key can flip direction # independently. # ---- Example 4: Hierarchical SOC -> PT sort with factor outer key ---- # # A factor outer key locks the SOC display order to the canonical # interleaved sequence (`overall` first, then `soc` blocks, then # `pt` detail rows inside each SOC) regardless of input order. what: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: One verb, one cascade. style() call appends single style_layer (location + style attributes) spec template. Layers accumulate declaration order; engine merges render time later layers win per attribute, NA-valued fields leave prior layer intact.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
code: style(.spec, ..., .at = cells_body())

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: .spec tabular_spec tabular_style_template. <tabular_spec | tabular_style_template>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind short attribute name ... spec slot. piping style_template() |> style(...) layers accumulate onto template instead spec. ... Named style attributes. least one required. See vocabulary list recognised names. .Location object selecting surface layer targets. <tabular_location>: default cells_body(). Build one cells_*() constructors; see cells_body() siblings. Dot-prefixed (tidyverse convention) comes ... — way user-passed style attribute can never collide arg's name.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: updated tabular_spec (tabular_style_template, called one).

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: Locations. argument selects surface layer targets. Every region rendered page cells_*() constructor: cells_body()             — body cells (default) cells_headers()          — column header band cells_group_headers()    — synthetic group-header rows cells_title()            — title block cells_subgroup_labels()  — subgroup banner row cells_footnotes()        — footnote block cells_pagehead()         — page-header band cells_pagefoot()         — page-footer band cells_table()            — table-wide regions (outer borders, body-row separators) Body filters live cells_body(): = 1:3 integer-index rows, j = "Total" column-name targeting, = <expr> quosure-captured predicate evaluated spec@data. Attribute vocabulary. layer carries style_node built .... Recognised attribute names: Text — bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_family, font_size Alignment — halign ("left" / "center" / "right"), valign ("top" / "middle" / "bottom") Borders — border (umbrella), border_top, border_bottom, border_left, border_right (takes brdr() value literal "none"); per-side scalars border_<side>_{style,width,color} finer control Padding — padding (scalar applies four sides; named vector c(top = , right = , bottom = , left = ) sets side); per-side scalars padding_<side> directly Spacing — blank_above, blank_below (integer blank lines / block — cells_title() / cells_footnotes() / cells_subgroup_labels()) Inline — pretext, posttext (literal text prepended / appended around cell value) Unknown attribute names emit cli::cli_warn drop constructed node; engine never sees foreign property.

# ---- House style via style_template() ---- house <- style_template() |> style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_headers()) |> style(border_top = brdr("thick"), .at = cells_headers()) |> style(border_bottom = brdr("thick"), .at = cells_headers()) |> style(border_bottom = brdr("medium"), .at = cells_table(side = "outer_bottom")) # Attach once via set_preset(); every tabular() chain inherits. # set_preset(style = house, font_size = 9)

what: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: Build reusable, composable style template chaining style() calls tabular_style_template. template carries ordered list style_layer records can attached preset() / set_preset() style = argument — every downstream tabular() chain inherits template's layers via engine cascade.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
code: style_template()  is_style_template(x)

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: x R object. predicate inspects class via inherits(); introspection performed.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: tabular_style_template — small S3 list layers slot. Pipe style() add layers.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: One verb, two surfaces. style(.spec_or_template, ..., .= ...) call attaches layer per-table spec also accumulates layers onto template. Symmetric API — need learn second function multi-table use case. Submission workflow. submission typically renders 100–200 tables one visual identity. Build template top submission script, pass set_preset(style = template), every subsequent tabular() produces output inherits column-header rules, group-header bolding, title spacing, outer-frame borders without single per-table style() call. Cascade order. Engines apply layers low--high priority: backend defaults → session preset's @style → spec preset's @style → per-spec style() layers. Later layers override prior ones per attribute; NA fields leave prior layer's value place.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
code: # ---- Sponsor "house style" composed once ---- # # The result becomes the default look for every table rendered # against this preset. No per-table style() boilerplate. house <- style_template() |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_headers(level = -1)) |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_group_headers()) |>   style(     border_top    = brdr("thick", "double"),     border_bottom = brdr("thick", "double"),     .at = cells_headers()   ) |>   style(blank_above = 1, blank_below = 1, .at = cells_title())  length(house$layers) #> [1] 4  # ---- Verify class ---- is_style_template(house) #> [1] TRUE

what: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: Attach subgroup_spec tabular_spec. render time engine partitions spec@data unique values , runs full resolve pipeline per group, concatenates results. hard page break inserted groups — every subgroup value starts page. centred banner line appears column-header rule every page group (including continuation pages), matching canonical submission page-layout convention.

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
code: subgroup(.spec, by, label = NULL)

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: .spec tabular_spec partition. <tabular_spec>: required. Column name(s) partition . <character>: required. Must reference column spec@data. Length-0 (character(0)) clears partition. Matches = arg convention sort_rows(). Multi-variable. Pass c("var1", "var2") cross every combination present data. Multi-var partitions require explicit label template (single-var auto-default generalise). label Banner template. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. Glue-style template {column_name} placeholders. NULL derives default partition variable's attr(data[[]], "label") (falling back column name). Tip: reference auxiliary columns inline BigN qualifier constant within group — e.g. "Cohort: {cohort} (N = {n})". Restriction: Every {col} reference must column spec@data. Unknown columns raise tabular_error_subgroup_template_unknown_col.

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining resolve via as_grid() / emit().

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: Label glue-style template. label carries {col} placeholders, engine substitutes placeholder FIRST ROW group's filtered data — column whose value constant within group (BigN, cohort descriptor, qualifier text) can ride banner. Columns vary within group also resolve, always first row's value; pre-compute aggregates upstream. Default label (label = NULL, single var): engine generates "<attr(data[[]], 'label') %||% >: {<>}", subgroup(= "cohort") renders banners like "Cohort: " "Cohort: B" without configuration. Replace, stack. second subgroup() call REPLACES prior partition — subgroup single spec, stackable list. Passing = character(0) clears slot, though typical clinical pipelines set partition front. Display-side . subgroup() partitions pre-summarised wide data frame; aggregate, filter, weight. user supplies one summary row per displayed row per group; tabular's job solely lay per-group banner page break. Multi-variable crossing. = c("SEX", "AGEGR1") partitions every combination present data (first variable varies slowest, matching expand.grid() convention). explicit label template required multi-var partitions since single-var default "<var>: {<var>}" generalise; raise tabular_error_subgroup_label_required otherwise. Auto-hide partition + template columns. Every column named , plus every column referenced via {col} placeholder label, automatically flips visible = FALSE engine time. Users restate col_spec(visible = FALSE) inside cols() columns — mirroring col_spec(indent_by = ...) auto-hide ergonomic.

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
code:
# ---- Example 1: TEAEs by treatment arm — one set of pages per arm ---- # # Partition the AE-by-SOC/PT pipeline by treatment arm. Each arm # value gets its own page set with a centred `Treatment Arm: ` # banner above the column-header rule on every page, separated by # hard page breaks. The default label uses the variable's `label` # attribute when present, falling back to the column name. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) attr(ae$row_type, "label") <- "Row Type" tabular( ae, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = "Drug 50", align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = "Total", align = "decimal") ) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) |> subgroup(by = "row_type") #> #>
#>

# ---- Example 2: Partition by Sex with inline BigN via template ---- # # `label` is a glue-style template; any column whose value is # constant within group can ride into the banner. `saf_subgroup` # ships partition-constant `sex_n` / `agegr_n` BigN columns # alongside the value cells, so each banner reads # `"Sex: F (N = 106)"`, etc. `sex` and `sex_n` auto-hide from the # body (partition `by` and template-referenced columns). tabular(saf_subgroup, titles = "Vital Signs at End of Treatment") |> cols( agegr = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Age Group"), agegr_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE), paramcd = col_spec(visible = FALSE), param = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"), stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"), placebo = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = "Drug 50", align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = "Total", align = "decimal") ) |> subgroup(by = "sex", label = "Sex: {sex} (N = {sex_n})") #> #>

# ---- Example 3: Multi-variable crossing (Sex x Age group) ---- # # Pass two columns to partition on every combination present in # the data. The label template MUST reference each variable # explicitly because the single-var auto-default does not # generalise. expand.grid order: first var (sex) varies slowest, # second (agegr) fastest, giving banner sequence F/<65, F/>=65, # M/<65, M/>=65. tabular(saf_subgroup, titles = "Vital Signs by Sex and Age Group") |> cols( sex_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE), agegr_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE), paramcd = col_spec(visible = FALSE), param = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"), stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"), placebo = col_spec(label = "Placebo", align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = "Drug 50", align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = "Total", align = "decimal") ) |> subgroup( by = c("sex", "agegr"), label = "Sex: {sex} / Age: {agegr}" ) #> #>

# ---- Example 4: Clear a partition with subgroup(character()) ---- # # `subgroup(by = character())` (or `subgroup(by = NULL)`) # explicitly clears any prior partition. Useful in # programmatically-built pipelines where a downstream branch # decides not to paginate by group after all — the call resets # the spec back to a single-page-set render. tabular(saf_subgroup, titles = "Pooled (no sex partition)") |> subgroup("sex", label = "Sex: {sex}") |> # Decide later that the sex split was the wrong default — # clear it before rendering. subgroup(character()) #> #>
