what: tabular: TFL Rendering for Regulatory Submissions — tabular-package
title: tabular: TFL Rendering for Regulatory Submissions — tabular-package
text: Native emission clinical submission tables RTF, LaTeX, HTML, PDF, DOCX. Handles decimal alignment via font metrics, multi-level column headers passthrough leaves, predicate-targeted styling, group-aware pagination. Designed CDISC ADaM workflows FDA / EMA / PMDA regulatory submissions.

title: tabular: TFL Rendering for Regulatory Submissions — tabular-package
text: Maintainer: Vignesh Thanikachalam .vignesh@gmail.com

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: Wrap pre-summarised data frame tabular_spec ready verb chain. tabular() entry verb — owns data, titles, footnotes slots; every downstream verb (cols(), headers(), sort_rows(), style(), paginate(), preset()) returns updated spec chaining, terminating emit() (write file) as_grid() (resolve without writing).

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
code: tabular(data, titles = NULL, footnotes = NULL)

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: data display rows. <data.frame>: required. Pre-summarised wide-format data; tibbles, data.tables, arrow tables coerced via .data.frame(). Factor columns preserved (levels drive sort_rows()). Restriction: least one column; column names must unique. Zero rows accepted (engine renders "data" stub). Interaction: cards-format counterparts (saf_demo_card, saf_aesocpt_card) accepted directly; pipe pivot_across() first. titles Page-title block, one element per row. <character> | NULL: default NULL. element renders centred line; embedded \n wraps within row. backend collapses unused rows column-header band sits flush lowest used title. Restriction: NAs.   footnotes Page-footnote block, one element per row. <character> | NULL: default NULL. User-supplied prose rows ; backend appends program-path / program-name / timestamp band render time. Restriction: NAs.
code: # Canonical 3-line title block with BigN-qualified population. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) titles = c(   "Table 14.3.1",   "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",   sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ) # Canonical 3-line footnote block. footnotes = c(   "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT.",   "Percentages based on N per treatment group.",   "TEAE = treatment-emergent adverse event." )

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: tabular_spec S7 object. Pipe cols(), headers(), sort_rows(), style(), paginate(), preset() build display, emit() render as_grid() resolve without writing.

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: Pre-summarised input contract. data one row per displayed row final table. tabular() aggregate, filter, weight, generate subtotal rows — happen upstream cards, dplyr, SAS. upstream long cards::ard_stack() ARD, pipe pivot_across() first land wide shape tabular() accepts. Multi-line titles footnotes contract. Clinical tables routinely carry 2-4 title rows 1-4 user footnote rows. Pass row one element character vector; backend renders element line, collapsing unused rows column-header band sits flush lowest used title.

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
code:
# ---- Example 1: Adverse-event table by SOC and Preferred Term ---- # # The regulatory work-horse layout: AE-by-SOC/PT with the # canonical 3-line title block (table number, description, # population qualifier with BigN drawn inline from `saf_n`) and a # two-line footnote block explaining the denominator. The # downstream pipeline hides the hierarchy markers (`row_type`, # `n_total`) but keeps them in the data so `sort_rows()` can # arrange SOCs and PTs in descending order of subject count. ae <- saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) tabular( ae, titles = c( "Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"]) ), footnotes = c( "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT.", "Percentages based on N per treatment group." ) ) |> cols( label = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"), soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"])), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"])) ) |> sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) #> #>
#>

# ---- Example 2: Best overall response with CDISC factor ordering ---- # # Efficacy table where response categories must appear in CDISC # clinical order (CR < PR < SD < NON-CR/NON-PD < PD < NE < # MISSING < ORR < DCR), not alphabetical. Coerce `stat_label` to # a factor with the canonical levels upstream and `sort_rows()` # picks up the order for free. bor_levels <- c( "CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "Objective Response Rate (CR + PR)", "Disease Control Rate (CR + PR + SD)" ) eff <- eff_resp eff$stat_label <- factor(eff$stat_label, levels = bor_levels) ne <- stats::setNames(eff_n$n, eff_n$arm_short) tabular( eff, titles = c( "Table 14.2.1", "Best Overall Response and Response Rates", sprintf("Efficacy Evaluable Population (N=%d)", ne["Total"]) ), footnotes = "Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment." ) |> cols( stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Response"), row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", ne["placebo"])), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", ne["drug_50"])), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", ne["drug_100"])) ) |> sort_rows(by = "stat_label") #> #>

title: tabular S7 classes — tabular_classes
text: S7 class definitions backing tabular's display-side IR. Users construct directly except col_spec(); every class built chained verb pipeline (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset() -> as_grid() / emit()).

title: tabular S7 classes — tabular_classes
text: class set intentionally small (~11 concepts) IR fits one mental model: Every spec slot typed: verb mutate slot invalid value fails construction time (S7 validator runs last-line defense behind cli-friendly verb-level validators). Class predicates. class matching is_<name>() predicate; see tabular_predicates full list.

what: Test for tabular S7 class instances — tabular_predicates
title: Test for tabular S7 class instances — tabular_predicates
text: Class predicates returning single logical indicating whether x inherits corresponding tabular S7 class. Use gate user-side code branches verb returned, write defensive helpers wrap tabular pipelines, assert intermediate shapes pipeline debugging.

title: Test for tabular S7 class instances — tabular_predicates
text: Eleven predicates cover full S7 surface: Predicates never error — return FALSE NULL, vectors, objects class, S7 objects packages. Use layer user's pipeline without defensive tryCatch().

# ---- Example 1: Gate user-side code on the spec class ---- # # A user-side helper that pre-validates its input before piping # into a downstream tabular chain. The predicate returns FALSE # for any non-spec input without raising, so the helper can emit # a friendlier error than tabular's own S7 validator would. add_safety_footnote <- function(spec) { if (!is_tabular_spec(spec)) { stop("`spec` must be a tabular_spec; build one with tabular().") } spec } demo <- tabular(saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") is_tabular_spec(demo) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE is_tabular_spec("not a spec") # FALSE — does not raise #> [1] FALSE add_safety_footnote(demo) #> #>

#>
#>
# ---- Example 2: Assert intermediate shapes during debugging ---- # # When chaining many verbs, dropping `stopifnot()` between verbs # gives a clear stack trace if a verb silently returns the wrong # type. Predicates are cheap (single S7 dispatch each) and never # error, so they are safe to leave in pipelines during dev. n <- stats::setNames(saf_n$n, saf_n$arm_short) spec <- tabular( saf_demo, titles = c("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics", sprintf("Safety Population (N=%d)", n["Total"])) ) |> cols( variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"), stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"), placebo = col_spec(label = sprintf("Placebo N=%d", n["placebo"]), align = "decimal"), drug_50 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 50 N=%d", n["drug_50"]), align = "decimal"), drug_100 = col_spec(label = sprintf("Drug 100 N=%d", n["drug_100"]), align = "decimal"), Total = col_spec(label = sprintf("Total N=%d", n["Total"]), align = "decimal") ) |> sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label")) stopifnot( is_tabular_spec(spec), is_col_spec(spec@cols[["placebo"]]), is_sort_spec(spec@sort) ) grid <- as_grid(spec) stopifnot(is_tabular_grid(grid))

title: tabular 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development. Public surface (verb signatures, S7 class shapes, error contracts) churn v0.1.0. file rewritten first tagged release; see git log live change history interim.

title: tabular 0.0.0.9000
text:
backend_html backend_md render resolved grid one continuous document instead one section per logical page. HTML backend emits single per horizontal panel one one ; vertical-page boundaries carried invisible markers fire page-break-: always @media print. Browsers natively repeat across resulting printed page breaks. MD backend emits one pipe table per horizontal panel body rows concatenated. paginate(repeat_headers = ...) paginate(continuation = ...) now ignored HTML MD backends — effective page-oriented backends (RTF / PDF / LaTeX / DOCX), per-printed-page headers continuation markers part regulatory output contract. Removed DOM / CSS classes HTML backend:
,
, comment,

paragraph, corresponding .tabular-page / .tabular-page-break / .tabular-continuation CSS rules. paginate(panels = N) HTML / MD output, panel renders table; HTML print single rule .tabular-table + .tabular-table forces subsequent panel onto new printed page.

title: tabular 0.0.0.9000
text: col_spec(indent_by = "<column_name>") declares per-row indent depth target column. referenced column carries integer (logical) values; engine prefixes target column’s text + AST row paste(rep(preset@indent_chars, depth), collapse = ""). Synthetic header rows group_display = "header_row" NEVER prefixed — stay flush parent depth 0. Typical clinical AE shape: Custom indent characters via preset(indent_chars = "    ") (4 spaces), preset(indent_chars = "> ") (custom marker), preset(indent_chars = "") (disable). depth column auto-hidden — need remember visible = FALSE . Works flat listings (group_display = "header_row" required) — column can declare indent_by drive per-row depth.
code: ae$indent_level <- as.integer(ae$row_type == "pt")  tabular(ae) |>   cols(     soc          = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row"),     label        = col_spec(label = "Category", indent_by = "indent_level"),     indent_level = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type     = col_spec(visible = FALSE)   ) # Overall + SOC summary rows stay flush; PT rows indent.

title: tabular 0.0.0.9000
text: PR-#2 default-behaviour (“every data row’s host-column text gets uniform indent_chars prefix group_display = "header_row" active”) removed. Indent now requires explicit col_spec(indent_by = "<col>") target column. Rationale: default-prefix mis-indented overall summary rows SOC summary rows regulatory AE pattern; new mechanism puts user control per row.

title: tabular 0.0.0.9000
text: preset_spec class drops five list-valued slots (@alignment, @borders, @fonts, @colors, @padding). named-list knobs still accepted preset() set_preset(), lower style_layer records preset_spec@style via .preset_args_to_layers() instead landing slot. Direct preset_spec(borders = list(...)) / preset_spec(fonts = list(...)) / etc. now raise “unused argument” — wrap chain tabular(...) |> preset(...) hit lowering. colors$border, colors$border_muted, colors$text_muted tokens rejected validation time. Use style(= cells_table(side = "rows"), border_top = brdr(color = ...)) (analogous outer / cols variants) table-wide stroke colour; style(= cells_footnotes(), color = ...) muted chrome text. Vector-form alignment (e.g. alignment = list(title_halign = c("left", "right"))) rejected. _halign / _valign key now accepts single character scalar. LaTeX backend longer emits table-wide preamble \definecolor{tabular_text}{HTML}{...} + \AtBeginDocument{\color{tabular_text}} block preset(colors = list(text = ...)) set. Per-cell \SetCell{...} carries colour via cells_style cascade. HTML backend longer emits .tabular-table td { color: ... } / .tabular-table { background: ... } / .tabular-table tbody td { padding: ... } CSS overrides preset(colors = ...) / preset(padding = ...) set. body cell’s inline style="..." attribute now carries colour / background / padding triple stamped cells_body() layer.
